description: Fossilised remains of half of Silurian brachiopod, Kirkidium knightii  showing septum
Fossilised remains of headshield of the Devonian cephalaspid  fish, Cephalaspis sp. found at Meadowley Hill. Headshield nearly complete in a course conglomerate matrix.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian heliotid tabulate coral, Plasmopora sp. The individual corallites are circular in outline and separeated by coenenchyme.
Fossilised remains of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras ibex, found at Mocktree.

Fossilised complete shell of the Silurian orthide brachiopod, Dalejina hybrida. The shell is symmetrical and equivalve and displays fine radial ribbing. Found at Buildwas. Mounted on card with G.00171, G.00173 and G.00174

Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophomena expansa, mostly in the form of internal moulds of single valves. Some original shell material. Also present are the Cricoconarid Tentaculites. Found by Bason, Miss at Cheney Longville
Fossilised Devonian plant remains found at Meadowley Wood.
Fossilised remains of single valves of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella unguis. Found at Cheney Longville, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Harknessella vespertilio. Believed to have been collected from the Craven Arms area.
Fossilised remains of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras ibex, found at Mocktree.
Fossilised shell fragments of the Silurian brachiopod Strophonella euglypha and bivalve Pterinea lineatula found at Bringewood.
Fossilised remains of a Devonian lung fish, Dipterus valenciennesi, showing a series of overlapping scales. Found at Caithness.
Fossilised remains of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found at Linney, Ludlow.
Fossilised fragments of the Ordovician cricoconarid Tentaculites anglicus. Found at Bromfield (washed downstream to Bromfield from Ordovician above Craven Arms)
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Sowerbyella sericea. Internal mould of single valves as well as external shell fragments.
Fossilised remains of an Incomplete cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites vulgaris, showing pronounced eyes. Found at Upper Millichope
Fragmentary fossilised remains, including scales, of the Devonian fish, Dipterus valenciennesi found at Acanarras Quarry, Spittal
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus intermedius found at Hillside,  Llandridod Wells
Fossilised shell fragment of the Silurian gastropod, Cyclonema octavia found at Norton Lane Quarry, Craven Arms, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite, Dalmanites myops. Showing well preserved crescentic eyes and glabella. Found at Upper Millichope, Shropshire.

Fossilised remains of fish Osteolepis sp. an extinct genus of lobe-finned fish from the Devonian period. It lived in the Lake Orcadie of northern Scotland. It was a rhipidistianand and had a number of features in common with tetrapods.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus corndensis found at Upper Gilwern, Howey
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina found at Linney, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras ibex cephalopod  found at Whitcliffe Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of segment of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense. Found in Wassall Quarry, Leintwardine.
Fossilised fragments of Silurian gastropod Loxonema sinuosa and trilobite Dalmanites myops, found at Upper Millichope
taxonomy: Loxonema sinuosa; Cyclonema sp.; Dalmanites myops

description: Fossilised remains of partial phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod Orthoceras nicholianum
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone from the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras ibex, showing septa. Found at Bringewood.
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras perversum. Mounted. Found at Dinham Bridge
Fossilised remains of the septum of the Silurian cephalopod, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense. Position of the hyponomic foramen visible on the septal surface.
Fossilised remains, including pygidium and part of thorax, of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus corndensis
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras perversum found at Linney below Ludlow Castle.
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Systrophoceras rapax. Oval whorl section and pronounced radial ribbing. Mounted on card.
Fossilised remains of fish scales of Palaeoniscum freieslebenensis in marl slate. Found at Quarington Quarry, Durham.

A fossilised fragment of Ludlow bone bed with a pinkish colour. Contains two sharp boundaries, one with a beige micrite and the other with a laminated pale grey siltstone. Towards the boundaries the bone bed becomes more yellow. Found at Ludford Lane
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus showing the cephalon, thorax and pygidium, found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised remains of the thorax and pygidium and partial cephalon of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus, found at Llanfawr quarry, Llandrindod Wells
Fossilised remains of Ordovician brachiopod, Strophomena grandis
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus, found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised remains of an almost complete individual of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus corndensis found at Upper Gilwern, Llandrindod Wells
Fossilised portion of the rib of a type of elephant from the Siwalik Hills of India.

Fossilised complete shell of the Silurian orthide brachiopod, Dalejina hybrida. The shell is symmetrical and equivalve and possesses fine radial ribbing. Found at Buildwas, Shropshire. Mounted on card with G.00171-00173
Fossilised remains of vertebra of an extinct species of crocodile. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ampyx sp. found at Hillside, Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised segment of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense. Found in Wassall Quarry, Leintwardine.
Fossilised piece of the lower jaw and a single tooth of an extinct type of horse from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras ibex, found at Mocktree.
Fossilised remains of a part of the lower jaw, including teeth, of an extinct type of antelope. From the Siwalik Hills of India.

Fossilised remains of a large sub-spherical septarian nodule, displaying polygonal cracks, which have been infilled with calcite.The nodule contains fragments of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone nautiloid, Orthoceras sp.  These internal moulds display clearly defined chambers, which have been partially infilled with mineral material. Found at Maryknoll.
Fossilised fragment of a rib bone from an extinct species of deer. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised piece of the lower jaw of an extinct giraffid. Collected from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Dolerorthis rustica.
Fossilised remains of an incomplete tooth of a Hippopotamus from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of lower palaeozoic fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devil's Hole, Morville
Fossilised remains of horse tooth from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Near complete fossilised remains of the head shield of a Pteraspid fish Cyathaspis sp. Found in Morville area.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised remains of the ventral disc from the head shield of the Silurian jawless fish, Phialaspis symondsi. Showing branchial impressions. Found at Devil's Hole, Morville Counterpart to G.03341.
Fossilised remains of a part of a jaw with teeth belonging to an extinct gharial type crocodile, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi found at Meadowtown.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian cephalopod Leurocycloceras whitcliffense showing the septal surface. Found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of part of Silurian eurypterid, Pterygotus punctatus
Fossilised remains of the Devonian jawless fish Pterapsis leathensis found at Morville.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian conularid Metaconularia aspersa found at Bow Bridge. Likely to be part of original Ludlow Natural History Society.
Fossilised remains of numerous cepahlons of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras bullatum cephalopod, showing septa.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian monoplacophoran Tremanotus sp. found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian eurypterid Pterygotus sp. found in the Main Channel, Section 2, Devils Hole.
Fossilised remains of forelimb of a juvenile Jurassic ichthyosaur. Numerous digit bones and two carpal bones preserved. Found at Lyme Regis.

Fossilised shell remains of a single valve of the Silurian bivalve, Cardiola interupta. Marked radial ribbing and concentric growth lines visible. Counterpart to G.04116.001. Mounted on card. Found at Mary Knoll House, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous coral Amplexus cotalbides.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Dawsonoceras annulatum, showing septal surfaces. The remains of a compound coral is on the reverse. Found in Much Wenlock district
Fossilised remains of the distal or lower end of the metacarpal bone of a deer from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Goniophora cymbaeformis found at Downton Castle.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian heterostracan fish Pteraspis leathensis.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Silurian trilobite, Dalmanites myops, found at Upper Millichope.
Internal mould showing part of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras bullatum. With fragments of Orbiculoidea sp. and Spirorbis sp. epizoic fossils.
Fossilised shell fragments of the Ordovician brachiopod Sowerbyella sericea found at Pont Derwlwyn, Llanfyllin, Montgomeryshire. Counterpart to G.04740.001
Fossilised shell of the Silurian gastropod Loxonema sinuosa found at Upper Millichope, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Upper Millichope, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Polygrammoceras bullatum, showing septal sututres. Found at Mill Street Weir, Ludlow.
Fossilized remains of the Devonian agnathan Pteraspis leathensis found at Meadowley Hill, Morville
Fossilised thorax and tail remains of the Silurian trilobite, Calymene nodulosa found at Burrington.
Fossilised remains of Silurian gastropod, Loxonema sp. Found at Sunnyhill, Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised fragment of spine from the Silurian Acanthodian fish Parexus sp. found at Devils Hole, Morville, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi preserving pygidium and partial thorax. Labelled as Ogygia sp. Found at Hillside.
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic ammonite Caloceras sp.
Septal surface of the Silurian cephalopod, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense, showing the position of the centrally placed septal foramen.
Fossilised fragment of the guard of the Cretaceous belemnite, Belemnitella mucronata. The guard is conical in form and possesses a conical alveolus. Mounted on card with five other specimens.
Fossilised remains of the distal or lower end of the metacarpal bone of a deer, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the ventral rim of a headshield belonging to a Devonian agnathan, Cephalaspis sp.

Fossilised remains of the limb bone of a Jurassic plesiosaur. This exhibits spectacular evidence of scavenging: not only 4 or 5 massive gouge marks on one side but on both sides, with matching orientation; also, both ends seem to have been gnawed away - these are not natural breaks.
Nodule containing the internal mould of the Silurian cephalopod, Gomphoceras sp. showing septa. Found in building stone in wall at Bromfield Manor. The counterpart of this specimen is G.05436.002.
Single valve of the Silurian fossil brachiopod, Ludfordina pixis and partial pygydia of the trilobite Dalmanites myops. Found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised remains of the stem section of the Carboniferous lycopsid, Sigillaria sp. Found at Highley Colliery, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of parts of Silurian graptolites, Pristiograptus sp. Found at the Sunken Road, Burrington.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian planospiral gastropod, Poleumita globulosa found at Bringewood. Counterpart to G.03988.001

Fossilised remains of the tail section of the Devonian lung fish, Dipterus sp., found at Achanarras Quarry, Spittal, Caithness. Individual scales, showing concentric growth rings, visible.Tail fin also preserved.
Silurian brachiopod, Orbiculoidea sp. Found near Ludlow Castle.
Fossilised footprints attributed to Cheirotherium in Triassic sandstone from Grinshill quarry
Fossilised remains of numerous Silurian graptolites, Pristiograptus sp., in a split and fractured nodule. Found in South Shropshire.
A fossilised internal mould of a strongly inequilateral valve of the Silurian bivalve, Modiolopsis laevis. Found at Upper Millichope.
A fossilised leaf of a Carboniferous seed fern, Neuropteris sp. Collected from the Coal Measures.
Silurian brachiopod Resserella elegantula, found at Buildwas.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Liospira sp.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian Graptolite Pristiograptus sp. Found at Leintwardine, Herefordshire
Fossilised remains of part of the Devonian plant, Parka decipiens, showing a reticulate structure. Found at Morville. Mounted on card.
Fossilised remains of a fern frond. Found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Kirkidium knightii. Found by Bayliss, J. at Haye Park near Ludlow
Fossilised shell remains of Silurian gastropod Cyclonema cirrhosa found at Sunnyhill Quarry near Ludlow, Shropshire
Internal mould of the valve of an Ordovician brachiopod, Dolerorthis sp.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula lewisi.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita sp. found at Mary Knoll, Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Fossilized remains of Tesseraspis sp.
Fossilised fragment of a jaw with teeth from an extinct animal species. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite, Dalmanites caudatus, showing glabella and right genal spine. Found in Sunken Road, Burrington.
Fossilised remains of Silurian plant fragments.
Fossilised fragments of Silurian gastropods, Platyschisma helicites. Found in South Shropshire.
Much Wenlock Limestone assemblage containing parts of Silurian Coenites sp.?, Coenites intertextus, Syringopora sp, Anastrophia deflexa?, Atrypa sp?. and Encrinurus sp.?. Found at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
taxonomy: Coenites sp.; Coenites intertextus; Syringopora sp.; Anastrophia deflexa; Atrypa sp.; Encrinurus sp.

description: Fossilised remains of Devonian fish Dipterus valenciennesi
Part of a Silurian fossil brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Dow, End Walsall
Fossilised remains of a Carboniferous pteridosperm, Alethopteris sp.  Part and counterpart with G14432.001.
Fossilised remains of a leaf stem of the Carboniferous pteridosperm, Neuropteris sp. Collected from the Coal Measures.
Fossilised valves of the Ordovician brachiopod Heterorthis alternata.
Assemblage of Silurian fossil brachiopods. Protochonetes ludloviensis, Salopina lunata and Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula found on Whitcliffe.
taxonomy: Protochonetes ludloviensis; Salopina lunata; Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula

description: Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Dawsonoceras annulatum. Showing partial septal surface.
Fossilised shell fragments of the Ordovician gastropod Bellerophon perturbatus found at Shelve, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the leaf whorl of the Carboniferous horsetail plant, Annularia stellata. Found in Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopods Amphistrophia funiculata (1) and Atrypa reticularis (2).
Fossilised remains of the ventral head shield of the fish Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devil's Hole.
Part of the ribbed phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Dawsonoceras annulatum.
Fossilised remains of Upper Carboniferous plants including Stigmaria rootlets, in water-worn ironstone nodules. Found at Sharpness Point, Tynemouth.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Leptostrophia filosa found  at Bringewood Chase.
Fossilized remains of the Devonian jawless fish Pteraspis leathensis found at Meadowley Wood.
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Kionoceras filosum. Found in the wall of a building in Leintwardine.
Fossilised remains of the cutwater spine of a Devonian Acanthodian fish found at Aston Hill Wood.
A series of complete and incomplete Jurassic ammonites, of the  Dactylioceras sp. The shells are serpenticone and evolute and in some examples show ribs which bifurcate across the venter.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician crinoid Iocrinus shelvensis. Includes upper part of stem, calyx and arms. Found by Norton, W.J. near Meadowtown, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras bullatum showing septal divisions in the septal wall. Found in Ludlow.
Abdomen of Silurian Phyllocarid, Ceratiocaris papilio.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Neodiversograptus nilssoni. Locality unknown.
A fossilised impression of a plant root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria sp. Circular scars mark the position of former rhizoids. Collected from the Lower Coal Measures.
Fragment of phragmocone of a Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil.
Fossilised fragments of the Ordovician bivalve Modiolopsis orbicularis found at Wistanstow, Shropshire.
Slab containing the fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobites, Cnemidopyge nuda and Ogygiocarella debuchi. Found nr Pen-y-bont, Radnorshire.

Fossilised fragment of the distal end of femur belonging to an extinct species of Bison. The upper shaft is missing. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India. An original label is attached to the shaft of the bone.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous plant root, Stigmaria, the giant club moss. Found at Woodhouse Colliery.
Silurian fossil cephalopod, Gomphoceras sp. from Mocktree, Leintwardine. Internal mould rounded in shape with visible chambers.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi found by Shuker, B. at Lower Farm, Betton.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Pterinea sp. found in the garden of St. Laurence School, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of imprint of fish scales of Palaeoniscum freieslebenisis on permian rock. Found in Durham County.
Fossilised remains of water-worn and rounded specimen containing numerous remains of  Ordovician cricoconarida, Tentaculites sp. Found at Bromfield.
Fossilised remains of complete specimen of the Ordovician trilobite Elrathia kingi.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Silurian trilobite, Dalmanites myops. Held within a matrix which includes bryozoa and brachiopod fragments.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites caudatus found at Sunken Lane, Burrington.

Fossilised remains of the cranium of an extinct type of rhinoceros. Though almost complete the cranium has been slightly squashed during preservation as viewed from the front. Found in the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of an Ordovician polyzoan. Found by Norton, W. J. at Soudley.
Fossilised remains of the Cambrian trilobite Olenus gibbosus found in North Wales.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous articulate brachiopod, Productus castatus
Fossilised remains of Neuropteris sp. leaves. Found in the East Shropshire Coalfield.

Fossilised remains of the left hand lower jaw of an extinct type of elephan. Found in the Siwalik Hills of India. A white rectangular area where original paper label was attached is visible on one side.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Monograptus priodon. Found at Bishop's Castle.
Near complete specimen of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi showing a good example of the hypostome. Found in the Llandrindod area.
A polished section of Carboniferous 'Encrinital Marble', showing fragments of indeterminate crinoid stems and ossicles. Found in Derbyshire.
Sandstone containing the fragmentary fossilised remains of Ordovician cricoconarida,  brachiopods and trilobites. Found at Marshbrook.
taxonomy: Tentaculites anglicus; Strophomena grandis; Dalmanella sp.; Trinucleus sp.

description: Fossilised shell remains showing the cast and mould of the Silurian bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of parts of Silurian graptolites, Monograptus sp., Found at Sutton. Counterpart of G.14792.002
Fossilised remains of the Single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Lingulella sp., Found at Hillside
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic crinoid Pentacrinites fossilis found at Charmouth.
Fossilised shell of Silurian gastropod Turbo sp. found in Mortimer Forest, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of cephalon showing the eye, glabellar and genial spines of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Loxonema sp. Found by Smith, R. at locality 33, Mortimer Forest, near Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of a leaf frond of the Carboniferous pteridosperm, Neuropteris sp. Collected from the Coal Measures.
Fossilized exoskelton of the Cretaceous echinoid (sea urchin), Echinocorys sp. Found in chalk deposits, Norfolk.
Part of the root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria. Deeply indented, ovoid scars, mark the position of former rhizoids.
Cretaceous flint nodule containing the fossilised remains of an internal siliceous mould of an echinoid (sea urchin). In 2 parts, see G.07763.001
Part of the root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria. Deeply indented ovoid scars mark the position of former rhizoids. Collected from the Upper Carboniferous.
Trace fossil possibly produced by the burrowing activity of marine annelids. Ordovician. Found at Pennerley.
Fossilised test of the Jurassic irregular echinoid, Plesiechinus ornatus. The test is flattened and shows clearly defined petaloid ambs. Found in Cheltenham.
Fossilised remains of a tooth from an extinct species of Bison. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian fish, Kallostrakan sp, with Acanthodian fin spine.
Fossilised fragment of long bone from an extinct unidentified species of mammal. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of numerous Silurian graptolites including Neodiversograptus and Monograptus sp. Found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian Hemicyclaspid fish found at Temeside, Ludlow.
Fossilised portion of the tusk of a type of elephant from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devil's Hole, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of long bone from an extinct unidentified mammal species. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of a root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria. Ovoid leaf scars, marking the position of former rhizoids are clearly visible.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian agnathan fish Hemicyclaspis murchisoni found at Temeside, Ludlow, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of atlas vertebra of a type of extinct elephant. Found in the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the ventral shield of the Devonian jawless fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devils Hole, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the articulate Ordovician brachiopod, Strophomena grandis. Also occurring within the specimen are conical fossils of Tentaculites sp. Found at Marshbrook, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of head shield of Silurian fish Hemicyclaspis sp. Preserved in a micaceous Silurian sandstone. Found at Temeside, Ludlow.

Fossilised remains of teeth of the extinct late Carboniferous shark-like fish Helicoptrion sp. in a siderite concretion. Teeth are arranged in spiral clusters called tooth whorls. Found in Shropshire.
Fossilized remains of the Cretaceous sea urchin Cidaris sp. Found at Charlton, Kent.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian fish Logania scotica found near Lesmahagow, Lanarkshire.
Part of the fossilised remains of the Devonian lung fish, Dipterus valanciennesi found in the Achanarras quarry, Spittal. Overlapping rows of scales and fins and fin spines well preserved.

Fossilised remains of spines from the Silurian Acanthodian bony fish Onchus sp. Also in the matrix are fossilised remains of the Silurian planispiral gastropod, Platyschisma helicites and the Silurian bivalve, Modiolopsis complanata. Found at Clun, Shropshire.
taxonomy: Onchus sp.; Platyschisma helicites; Modiolopsis complanata

description: Fossilised remains of part of the head shield of the Silurian Cephalaspid fish, Hemicyclaspis sp. plus fragments of Acanthodian spines.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian agnathan fish Cephalaspis sp. found at Oak Dingle
Fossilised remains of the foliage of a Carboniferous seed fern, Cyclopteris sp. Found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the shoulder girdle and prepectoral spine of a fish, Climatius sp.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian jawless cephalaspid fish Benneviaspis salopiensis found at Besom Farm Quarry.
Rostrum of an early palaeozoic fish, Pteraspis crouchi.
Fossilised fragment of a spine from a Silurian bony-jawed, Acanthodian fish found at Temeside, Ludlow.
Fossilised leaves and stem fragment of a Carboniferous horsetail. Annularia sp. Found at Longden Common.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian ancanthodian fish Dickosteus sp. found at Dippel Brae.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilized remains of the Silurian fish Kallostrakon sp. found at Foxall Coppice, Monkhopton.
Fossilised remains of a Devonian fish
Fossilised shell fragments of the Silurian bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina.
Part of the fossilised remains of a Devonian lung fish, Dipterus valanciennesi, found at Achanarras quarry, Spittal. Displays rows of overlapping scales.
Fossilised remains of the shoulder girdle of an Acanthodian fish. Found at Cadbol.
Partially preserved median oral plate of the Palaeozic fish Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devil's Hole.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Mytillus sp.
Fossilised remains of part of a frond of a Carboniferous tree fern, Pecopteris sp. Veined pinnae are well preserved.
An evolute Jurassic ammonite, Kosmoceras acutistriatum, with strong ribs which bifurcate towards the venter. Evidence of tubercles along the ventral margin.
Fossilised remains of scales, spines and fin material from the Devonian lobe finned fish, Osteolepis macrolepidotus found at Quoyloo Quarry, Orkney.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found in Vicarage Road, Builth Wells.

Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Ordovician trilobite Telaeomarrolithus intermedius. Two specimens are preserved in the matrix. Found at Llandrindod. Scarce specimens showing good fossilised cephalon. Identified by Botting and Muir, March 2017.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous plant, Annularia sp., found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of Jurassic ammonites, found at Kimmeridge, Dorset.
Fossilised remains of ventral rim of the head shield of a Silurian fossil fish, Hemicyclaspis lightbodii. Found at Temeside, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of a molar tooth from an extinct species of elephant, Stegodon sp. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
A fossilised fragment of the jaw of a Silurian, Acanthodian bony fish.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian phyllocarid, Ceratiocaris papilio. Found near Lesmahagow, Lanarkshire.
Fossilised remains of Jurassic ammonites, preserved in Kimmeridge Clay and found at Kimmeridge Bay, Dorset.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobolite Ampyx linleyensis found at Nind Quarry.

A near complete planispiral phragmocone of a Jurassic ammonite. The phragmacone is evolute and displays some typical ammonitic suture lines. On the venter there is a strong keel, with a sulcus on either side.
Fossilized remains of the Devonian fish Pteraspis rostrata found at Hudwick Dingle.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian trilobite Calymene blumenbachi in its curled up form showing parts of the cephalon, thorax and pygidium.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Poluemita sp. Found at Walcott.

Ordovician mudstone from the Caradoc series containing the graptolite Climacograptus. Also present is a well preserved skeleton of the sponge Cyathophycus loydelli as well as incomplete trilobites - Cnemidopyge nuda and some small, poorly preserved brachiopods.
taxonomy: Climacograptus brevis brevis; Homalopteon murchisoni; Cnemidopyge nuda; Cyathophycus loydelli

description: Silurian trace fossils Planolites and Chondrites, probably produced by burrowing annelids. Found by Krause, J. and Norton, W. J. at Gullet Quarry.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian heterostracan fishTraquairaspis symondsi found at Little Oxenbold.
Fossilised remains of the  internal mould of a fragment of the stem of the Carboniferous crinoid, Rhodocrinus sp. Commonly described as 'screwstone'. Found in Millersdale, Derbyshire.
Assemblage of Silurian fossils including the remains of the cricoconarid Tentaculites sp. Found by Phillips, N.M. at Wren's Nest.
Fossilised remains of theSilurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found in Elton Lane.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian eurypterid, Pterygotus bilobus. Found at Lesmahagow.
Fossil of the branchial plate of the palaeozoic fish Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devil's Hole. Preserved in Limestone. Counterpart to G.08153.002
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic brittle star Ophioderma egertoni. Found at Cleatham, Lincolnshire.
Fragmentary remains of the Silurian trace fossil Keilorites squamosus. Found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. Found at Church Hill near Leintwardine.
Much Wenlock Limestone containing fossilised parts of Silurian stromatoporoids, Favosites sp., Heliolites sp. and a crinoid columnal.
Dorsal ridge of the Devonian fish Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devile's Hole.
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic ammonite, Echioceras sp.
Sample of fine grained precambrian sandstone showing fine sedimentary banding. Found near Wentnor, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragments of Silurian graptolites, Neodiversograptus nilssoni, found at Cilmery, Builth Wells.

Silurian assemblage with brachiopods Salopina lunata, Camaratoechia nucula, Protochonetes ludloviensis and Orbiculoidea rugata, a polyzoan, bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina,  Nuculites sp. and septum of cephalopod Orthoceras sp.  Found at Whitcliffe Quarry, Ludlow.
taxonomy: Salopina lunata; Camaratoechia nucula; Protochonetes ludloviensis; Orbiculoidea rugata; Polyzoan; Fuchsella amygdalina; Nuculites sp.; Orthoceras sp.

description: Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found at Guilsfield.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Murchisonia sp. found by Rowlands, M., at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
Precambrian fine grained sandstone with ripple marked bedding surface. Found near Wentnor, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of ribbed phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Systrophoceras rapax.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian fossil bryozoa, Ptilodictya lanceolata and brachiopods. Protochonetes ludloviensis, found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian graptolites Neodiversograptus nilsoni and Monograptus sp. Found in Mortimer's Forest, near Ludlow.
Small sample of precambrian fine grained sandstone showing fine sedimentary banding. Found near Wentnor, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian sponge Spongarium edwardsi; brachiopods Orbiculoidea sp. and cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found at Linney, Ludlow.
taxonomy: Spongarium edwardsi; Orbiculoidea sp.; Orthoceras sp.

description: Fossilised fragment of phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras sp. Specimen in 4 parts - part 2. Matrix also contains fossils of brachiopods.
Fossilised remains of an ammonite, Ludwigella sp. that has been sectioned to reveal the inner structure.
Fossilised remains of Silurian bryozoan found at High Vinnals, Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian hyolithid found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of Silurian calcareous algae, Pachytheca sp. Found at Mortimer Forest.
A series of fragmented carbonised Devonian plant remains.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian sponge, Spongarium edwardsi sp. found at Linney, Ludlow.
Silurian tabulate coral Favosites sp. Found at Shadwell Quarry, near Much Wenlock.
Fossilised remains of a Cretaceous brachiopod - Terebratula (?dutempleana d'Orbigny), very well preserved, with both halves still connected, and surface markings clearly visible.
A carbonised fragment of the Devonian plant, Parka decipiens, showing a reticulate structure. Mounted on card.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita funatus found by Wood, B.J. at Farley Quarry.
Silurian assemblage containing fossilised remains of graptolite Diplograptus sp.; a polyzoan and brachiopods. Found at Bromfield, transported in alluvial gravels.
Silurian fossil brachiopod, Protochonetes ludloviensis
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic Asteroceras sp. ammonite fossils with bivalve and belemnite fragments also in the matrix.. Found in Evesham.
Fragment of the Silurian Cephalaspid fish, Hemicyclaspis sp. showing a series of bony plates. Fragment of an Acanthodian fish spine also present.
Fine grained precambrian sandstone showing fine sedimentary banding
Fossilised skeletal remains of the Palaeogene, ray finned, bony fish, Myripristis leptacanthus. Found on Monte Bolca, Verona, Italy.
Parts of Silurian fossil brachiopods. Protochonetes ludloviensis, Salopina lunata and Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula found in Tinker's Hill Wood, Saltmoor
taxonomy: Protochonetes ludloviensis; Salopina lunata; Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula

description: Fossilised remains of several of the Jurassic ammonites, Dactylioceras commune, in a nodule. The shell is evolute and serpenticone with a shallow umbilicus. Ornamentation occurs as fine ribs, some of which bifurcate across the venter.
Fossilised remains of a single vertebra from an extinct species of deer. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fine grained buff-coloured Ordovician sandstone.
Partially preserved remains of a fossilised fin spine of the palaeozoic fish Onchus sp.
Fossilised remains of headshiels of the Devonian jawless heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi.
Silurian fossil brachiopods, Shagamella ludloviensis found at Onibury.
Fossilised assemblage of Silurian brachiopods, Salopina lunata, Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula and Protochonetes ludloviensis found at Whitcliffe Quarry, Ludlow
taxonomy: Salopina lunata; Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula; Protochonetes ludloviensis

description: Grey brown conglomerate with semi-rounded rock fragments in dense matrix. Found at Haughmond Hill - the track section.
Fossilised remains of brachial shoulder girdle of the Devonian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian fish Pteraspis sp.
Silurian fossil brachiopods, Shagamella sp., Dayia navicula, Isorthis orbicularis and Atrypa reticularis found at Downton Castle
taxonomy: Shagamella ludloviensis; Dayia navicula; Isorthis orbicularis; Atrypa reticularis

description: Part of Silurian phyllocarid, ceratiocaris papilio.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian stromatoporoid, Stromatopora sp., found at Farley.
Fossilised remains of a Devonian jawless heterostracan fish.
A polished section through the phragmacone of an ammonite. The phragmacone is planispiral and evolute. Septal walls are coated with crystalline material which completely fills some chambers.
Fossilized fragment of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. showing chamber sections in the phragmocone. Specimen in 4 parts - part 3. Found at Tinkers Hill 1925
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Rhynchonella sp. found on Wenlock Edge.
Part of Silurian sponge, Salpinostoma sp.
Fossilised fragments of carbonised Devonian plants.
Fossilised fragments of indeterminate Devonian plant remains found at Targrove Quarry, Downton Hall, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian Aganthan fish Hemicylaspis sp.
Part of a leaf frond from the Carboniferous seed fern, Seftenbergia sp.
Silurian fossil brachiopods, Protochonetes ludloviensis.
Fossilised fragment of branched stem of the Silurian plant, Prototaxites sp. found at Hopton Brook.
Fossilised remains of the lower jaw, with molar and premolar teeth, of an extinct type of antelope. From the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fragmentary remains of the spine of a Devonian fish, Onchus sp. Found in a sandstone matrix at Cusop.
Fossilised remains of a leaf frond of the Carboniferous pteridosperm, Neuropteris sp.
Silurian fossil brachiopods, Chonetes lepisma found above Dayia navicula on Brand Hill.

A complete specimen of the Jurassic Bivalve, Gryphaea arcuata. The shell  is typically inequivalved, with the left valve possessing an incurved umbo, whilst the right valve is flat. Collected from a well sinking at Wollaston, near Whitchurch, at a depth of twenty two feet.
Fossilised remains of a part of the upper jaw, with molar teeth, of an extinct type of deer from the Siwalik Hills of india.
Part of a fin spine, and other fragmented remains, from a Devonian fossil fish.
Triassic fine-grained sandstone with ripple mark impressions. Found at Grinshill Quarry, Shropshire.
Part and counterpart of a Silurian cephalopod.  Found at Mocktree Quarry, Shropshire 1925. Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena sp. also present.
Fossilised distal or lower end of the metacarpal of a deer from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian plant, Powysia bassettii
Modiolopsis complanata bivalve fossil(s) with ostracod fossils, both from the Silurian.
Fine-grained sandstone, showing clearly defined fossilised ripple marks. The ripple marks are asymmetric in form. Collected from the Triassic, Tarporley Siltstones at Grinshill Quarry.
Fossilised head shield of a Silurian fish, Hemicyclaspis sp. Found at Case Mills, Ludlow.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina found at Bringewood, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Monograptus priodon, Found by Phillips, N.M.
Part of the shell of the Silurian articulate brachiopod, Leptaena sp. Clearly defined radial ribbing, which is intersected by equally fine concentric growth lines.
Partially preserved jaw of an acanthodian fish.
Fossilised remains of part of Silurian graptolite Monograptus sp., Found by Shepherd, H. H. at Climery
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found on Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised piece of a deer's lower left jaw, with teeth, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of headshield of the Silurian Cephalaspid fish Sclerodus cornua found at Bradnor Hill on the Welsh Border.
A large carbonised fragment of a Devonian fossil plant.
Fossilised remains of Silurian brachiopod Microsphaeridiorynchia nucula found in Ludlow.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian plant, Powysia bassettii. Found by Smith, R. at Church Hill, Leintwardine
Fragment of fossilised horn from an extinct species of bovid. From the Siwalik Hills of India. in 2 parts - G.10029.002
Fossilised polished section through the Devonian tabulate coral Pachypora (Thamnopora) sp. Individual corallites are sub circular and small. Mounted on card.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Sphaerirhynchia wilsoni found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras bullatum found at Ludford Park.
Fossilised pygidium of the Silurian trilobite, Hemiarges sp. found at Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised root, Stigmaria, from a Carboniferous lycopod. Circular scars mark the position of former rhizoids.
Fossilised part of a Silurian brachiopod, Cyrtia exporrecta found at Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Pentamerus oblongus found in Shropshire
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Cyrtoceras laevis.
Fossil of the head shield of the jawless Silurian fish, Sclerodus pustuliferous. Two well developed spines can be seen, one on either side of the shovel shaped head shield.
Fossilised stem, Sigillaria sp. of a Carboniferous lycopod. Very strong longitudinal ridges visible.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Atrypa reticularis found at Rubery.
Fossilised remains of the foliage of a Carboniferous seed fern, Cyclopteris sp., found in the East Shropshire Coalfield.
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Cyrtolites laevis.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena  sp. found at  Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised remains of part of a Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa hemisphaerica found at Rubery
Fossilised stem, Sigillaria sp, from a Carboniferous lycopod. Strong longitudinal ridges and small elipsoidal leaf scars, visible
Fossilised remains of a complete Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Bringewood
Fossilised segment of appendage from a Silurian Eurypterid, Pterygotus sp., found at Brandon, Leimtwardine. Counterpart to G.0451.001
Fossilised piece of a deer's lower left jaw, with teeth, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
A block of fine-grained sandstone with ripple marks and other trace fossils. Found at Grinshill Quarry.
Part of a leaf frond from a Carboniferous seed fern. Found at Longden Common.
Valve of a Silurian fossil brachiopod, Leptaena contermina. Preserved alongside fragments of Tentaculites. Found in Hope Quarry
Fossilised remains of the Cephalon of the Ordovician trilobite Onnia superba. Found by Ellis, R. A. beside river Onny at Wistanstow.
Fossilised remains of a part of the skull and teeth of an un-identified type of fossil elephant from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian brachiopod, Gypidula dudleydensis. Found by Pardoe, H.  Part of the original Ludlow Natural History Society collection.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian graptolite, Pristiograptus sp. Collected by Smith, R. D. A. at Leintwardine.
Complete exoskeleton of an Ordovician trilobite in a fine-grained sandstone matrix. Found at Marshbrook.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Pterinea lineatula found at High Vinnalls, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of a headshield of Cephalaspid fish. Found at landslip site, Cusop.
Fossilised remains of a nearly complete specimen of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi.

Fossilised remains of exoskeleton of the Ordovician trilobite, Flexicalymene quadrata. The cephalon is particularly detailed, with clearly defined furrowed glabella and compound eyes. Found at Bushmoor.  Identified by Botting and Muir.
Fossilised remains of the conical corallum, of the Silurian solitary, rugose coral, Ketophyllum subturbinatum. Found at Wenlock Edge, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Imbricatoceras subundulatum showing septal divisions in the septal wall. Found at Burrington.
Part of the jaw of a Silurian Ischacanthid fish. Part and counterpart with G.09946.002
Fossilised remains of a Silurian starfish found at Chuch Hill, Leintwardine
Fossilised rhabdosome of the Ordovician dendroid graptolite, Clonograptus tenellus.The rhabdosome is branching with uniserial stipes. Found at Chermes Dingle, Wrekin, Shropshire.
Fossilised assemblege of shell remains of the Silurian articulate brachiopod, Kirkiduim knightii, showing strong radial ribbing. Found at Onibury, Shropshire.
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Lituites ibex.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous plant Cordaites sp. with lycopod fragments and pteridosperms on the reverse. Found at Longden Common, Shrewsbury.
Part of phragmocone of a Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil
Fossilised valve of the Silurian brachiopod Glassia sp. Found at Shadwell Quarry.
A counterpart to a fossilised Silurian  assemblage of brachiopods,  Salopina lunata,  Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula and Protochonetes ludloviensis found at Whitcliffe Quarry, Ludlow
taxonomy: Salopina lunata; Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula; Protochonetes ludloviensis

description: Fossilised remains of tooth of the Devonian bony Coelacanth Crossopterygian fish. Found at West Portished.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Cyrtoceras laevis found at Hope, Dale, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus corndensis, found at Upper Gilwern, Howey,  Llandrindod Wells

Grey-black coarse-grained igneous Gabbro rock with basaltic composition. Composed of pale green feldspar and black pyroxene. Collected from an intrusion on the Lawley Hill, near Church Stretton, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Trigonirhynchia stricklandii, found on Wenlock Edge, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of headshield of the Silurian jawless Heterostracan fish Tesseraspis sp. found at Little Oxenbold.
Part of the exoskeleton of the Ordovician trilobite, Ampyx linleyensis. The pygidium is near complete, whilst the thorax and cephalopn are incomplete. Collected from the Shelve inlier.
Fossilised remains of cephalon, thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops. Cephalon clearly showing eyes, glabella and genial spines. Found at Upper Millichope, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Rhynchonella sp. found on Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian trilobite Encrinurus sp. found on  forestry road in Mortimer Forest

Fossilised remains of the rhabdosome of the Ordovician graptolite, Dicellopgraptus cambriensis. The rhabdosome consists of two uniserial reclined stipes. Mounted on card with G.11148.001 and G.11148.003
Fine-grained sandstone from the Grinshill Flagstone member of Grinshill Quarry. Shows sedimentary structures including adhesion ripples.
Fossilised frond of the Carboniferous pteridosperm Mariopteris sp., found in Snowdonia.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at High Vinnals, Mortimer Forest near Ludlow.
A fine-grained, well sorted quartz rich Lydebrook Sandstone. White in colour with some secondary iron staining. Found at Coalbrookdale.
Fossilised horse tooth from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ectillaenus pervolus found in Hope stream. an unidentified gastropod is also in the shale matrix.
Fossilised shell remains of Silurian bivalve Goniophora cymbaeformis found at Ludford

Fossilised remains of an assemblage of Ordovician brachiopods including Dalmanella sp, Heterorthis alternata, Harknessella sp. and Sowerbyella sericea. Also present are fragments of trilobites including Trinucleus sp. Found by Norton, W.J. at Soudley Quarry.
taxonomy: ['Dalmanella sp.', 'Heterorthis alternata', None, 'Sowerbyella sericea', 'Trinucleus sp.', 'Brongniartella sp.']

description: Specimen of beige-grey limestone, consisting of many (up to 30 mm diameter) cannon ball shaped pieces cemented together.
Fossilised remains of the pygidium, and part of the thorax, of the Silurian trilobite, Dalmanites myops. The pygidium is terminated by a spine.
A fine-grained Sandstone, collected from within the Triassic Tarporley Siltstones at Grinshill Quarry. Showing well-defined ripple marks.
Fossilised remains of single fragmented valves of the Ordovician brachiopod Macrocoelia expansa. Preserved as original shell material. Found by Hay, T. in Marshbook area.
Fossilised shell remains of the Ordovician gastropod Sinuites sp. found at Lower Wood Brook, Meadowtown, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone of an Ordovician cephalopod Cyrtoceras sp. with a fragment of Ogyginus sp trilobite, collected by Tarrant, P, Norton, J.  & Norton, M., in Priestweston Quarry
Fossillised remains of a cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops, coral, Coenites sp. and a crinoid columnal. Found at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula minima and fragments of eurypterid found in Hermits Cave, Downton Castle.
Fossilised remains of Neuropteris sp. (?) leaf stem.

Fossilised, near complete phragmocone of the Carboniferous goniatite, Gastrioceras carbonarium. The phragmocone is involute and the aperture sphaerocone. Fine radial ribs extend across the venter. Collected from the Coal Measures.
Fragmentary remains of the Ordovician trilobolite Dionide turnbulli. From West Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of cephalon showing glabella and right genial spine of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites caudatus found at Sunken Road, Burrington.
A fine grained, vesicular, mesocratic volcanic rock. Collected from the Precambrian Uriconian rocks of Caer Caradoc, Shropshire.
Jurassic bivalve
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophonema grandis. Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised remains of several Ordovician trilobites, Ogygiocarella debuchii. Found at Meadowtown Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian fossil brachiopod, Glassia sp., found at Upper Millichope

Greenish-grey coarse-grained Ordovician Dolerite, igneous rock, similar to basalt, mostly consisting of plagioclase feldspar (green and white) and pyroxene (black) crystals. Collected from Squilver Quarry.
Fossilised shell remains of the Cretaceous bivalve Inoceramus sp.

Fossilised remains of the internal mould of ventral valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophonema grandis. Also preserved are fragmentary remains of bryozoa and Tentaculites. Found by Norton, W.J. at Marshbrook.

Fossilised remains of a near complete shell of the Carboniferous articulate brachiopod, Productus punctatus. Found at Wensleydale, Yorkshire. The gently concave brachial valve shows fine radial ribbing and concentric growth lines. The pedicle valve is convex, has a shallow sulcus, and shows corrugated, concentric growth lines.
Fossilised remains of the horn shaped corallite of the Cretaceous solitary coral Trochocyathus conulus showing the septal furrows and growth lines. From the Upper Greensand formation.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Harnessella subquadrata. Found at Harnage Grange.

A medium grained, melanocratic, porphyritic igneous rock, containing phenocryts of both olivine and feldspar. Collected from the Pontesford/Earls Hill intrusion, which is part of the Uriconian Volcanic Group.
Fossilised remains of the rounded, segmented thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Homalonotus sp.
Fossilised fish fragments from the Cretaceous Period, found at Hanover point, Isle of Wight.
Lower carboniferous limestone with fragmentary remains of starfish and crinoids. Found near Kielder.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Heterorthis alternata. Found at Soudley Quarry.
Fossiled ripple marks on Precambrian purple shales. Collected from the Longmynd, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Cenozoic gastropod, Cerithium sedgwickii. Part of the Charles Ashford Collection.
Sole markings in carboniferous sedimentary rock found at Granville Colliery, Shropshire.
Fossilised remins of an asymmetric valve from a Carboniferous bivalve, found in North Shrophire. Fine radial ribbing and concentric growth lines clearly visible.
Fossilised remains of the Lower Carboniferous compound Rugose coral, Lithostrotion sp. showing a phaceloid arrangement of the corallites.
Remains of an assemblage of Ordovician fossils including the brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Also tentaculites, and trilobite fragments. Found in Church Stretton parish area.
Ripple marks in Precambrian Longmyndian rock. Found in Cardingmill Valley, Church Stretton, Shropshire.
Internal mould of the shell of a Jurassic bivalve, Laevitrigonia gibbosa. Found at Portland, Dorset.
Fossilised remains of thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Calymene sp. and phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found in Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous conularid Conularia quadrisulcata found at Coalbrookdale, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the internal mould of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Found by Stace, H.E. at Minton.

A derived Jurassic fossil belemnite guard. Collected from Quaternary boulder clay, near Stevenage. The guard tapers towards the posterior end, whilst an alveolus can be found at the anterior end. Mounted on card.

Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella unguis. Preserved as internal and external moulds in an assemblage that includes fragments of trilobites and Tentaculites sp. Found by Baker, H. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous starfish, Neupalaeaster sp. found at Plashetts Quarry, Kielder, Northumberland
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Harknessella sp. Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
Microscope thin section of Camptonite, made from a hand specimen collected at Maddocks Hill.
Fossilised and pyritised remains of a Jurassic ammonite. The shell is evolute and possesses a shallow umbilicus. Fine transverse ribs, with tubercles, extend across the smooth venter.
Fossilised single valve of a Jurassic bivalve shell
Fossilised remains of water-worn Silurian assemblage found at Brittal Quarry, Richard's Castle
Fossilised shell remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Heterorthina notata. Found at Min-y-dwr, Llanyblodwel

Fossilised remains of the internal mould of the Jurassic ammonite, Amoeboceras sp. The shell is compressed and involute and possesses a deep umbilicus. The shell is deeply etched, revealing ammonitic suture. A derived fossil from the Jurassic Ampthill Clay and collected from the Lowestoft Till.
Fossilised fragment of guard of the Cretaceous belemnite, Belemnitella mucronata. The guard is conical in form and possesses a conical alveolus. Mounted on card with five other specimens.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Goniophora cymbaeformis.

Fossilised complete shell of the Silurian orthide brachiopod, Dalejina hybrida. The shell is symmetrical and equivalved and displays fine radial ribbing. Found at Buildwas, Shropshire. Mounted on card with G.00172-G.00174
An assembledge of fossilised shell valves and moulds of the Silurian brachiopod, Microsphaeridiorhynchus nucula. An original Ludlow Natural History Society specimen. From Whitelciffe, Ludlow
Fossilised remains of bone bed containing Silurian fish fragments including scales, bone and spine. Found at Onibury, Shropshire.
Medium grained sandstone containing a mixture of internal moulds and original shell material of the Ordovician brachiopods Dalmanella wattsi and Kjaerina sp., found on the A49 road
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras ludense showing the septal structure.
Microscope thin section of Precambrian Longmyndian Greywacke, made from a hand specimen collected at Bayston Hill.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Discina striata found in Wigmore Road Ludlow. Original Ludlow Natural History Society specimen.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula lewisi found in the Malvern district. Part of the Bibbs Collection.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Platycalymene duplicata, found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita discors found at Pipe Aston, near Ludlow
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula striata found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Turbo sp.
Fossilised remains of Internal mould of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Kjaerina typa. Found at Acton Scott.

Internal mould of a large fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone nautiloid, Orthoceras bullatum. Straight suture lines, marking the septal walls, well preserved. Brachipod fossils also present.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita globosa. Found at Diddlebury.
Fossilised remains of a tusk of a Hippopotamus, that has been cut and polished on one surface to show the internal structure. Collected from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Palaeopecten danbyi found in Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian cephalaspid fish Cephalaspis sp. found at Bromfield, Shropshire.

A near complete fossilised exoskeleton of the Ordovician trilobite, Trinucleus gibbsi. The cephalon has an inflated glabella with a pitted margin, and extends into a well preserved genal spine. Identified by Botting and Muir.
Fossilised remains of Devonian agnathan Pteraspis rostrata with Cephalaspid cornu found at Hudwick Dingle, Monkhopton
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita rugosa found at Farley Quarry, Much Wenlock
Fossilised fragment of lower jaw, including teeth, from an extinct species of bison. From the Siwalik Hills, India
Fossilised remains of Ordovician trilobite, Ogygiocarella debuchi, from Hillside, near Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised remains of partial specimen of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus corndensis, from Upper Gilwern near Llandrindod.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Ordovician trilobites Platycalymene duplicata and Trinucleus fimbriatus, found at Llanfawr Quarry, LLandrindod Wells
Fossil remains of a Silurian gastropod, Loxonema sinuosa, preserved as an internal mould and cast. Also preserved is a valve of the bivalve Pterinea sp. and fragments of brachiopod and bryozoan.
Fossilised remains of part of the upper jaw of an extinct antelope with teeth. Collected from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of  the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus found at Llanfawr quarry, Llandrindod Wells
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus sp. found by Birmingham University Extra Mural Department at Meadowtown Quarry.
Fossilized remains of Devonian agnathan Pteraspis sp. found in Aston Hill Wood
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena depressa found at Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula striata found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised fragments of a cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanities myops (KÃ¶nig) showing glabella, eyes and genial spines. Found at Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian trilobite Phacops stokesi found at Upper Millichope, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobites Trinucleus fimbriatus and Platycalymene duplicata, found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras ibex showing septa. Found at Bringewood.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Atrypa orbicularis found at Leintwardine

Fossilised remains of both valves of the Cretaceous Bivalve, Spondylus spinosus. The shell is equivalved and equilateral and the valves are attached at the umbo (dorsal) end, and gaping at the anterior margin.. The exterior ornament is made up of strong radial ribs, to which small tubercles are attached. Infilled with chalk.
Fossilised leaf fragments of the Carboniferous plants, Pecopteris sp. and Alethoptens sp. Found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the central section of the metacarpal bone of a deer from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilized fragment of an extinct species of cephalopod, possibly Orthoceras sp. found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow. The specimen also contains fossilised fragments of Silurian brachiopod shells.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian cephalopod Kionoceras angulatum and bivalve Orthonota sp. found at Aymestry, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchii found at Meadowtown.
Fossilised remains of stem of the Carboniferous ' horsetail ', Calamites sp. Found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the proximal end of a deer femur from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Strophonella euglypha.
Fo0ssilised fragment of guard of the Cretaceous belemnite, Belemnitella mucronata. The guard is conical in form and possesses a conical alveolus. Mounted on card with five other specimens.

Fossilised remains of a strongly ribbed, moderately evolute phragmocone of the Cretaceous ammonite, Euhoplites sp. A pronounced sulcus occurs on the venter and tubercles can be found both on the umbilical margin and the edges of the sulcus.
Fossilised leaf fronds of the Upper Carboniferous pteridosperm, Neuropteris sp. -  part one of two, of a fractured specimen
Fossilised remains of pygidium, thorax and part of the cephalon of the Silurian trilobite, Tapinocalymene nodulosa. Found at Brimfield.
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense.
Fossilised section of stem of the Carboniferous Lycopod plant, Lepidodendron sp. showing leaf scars. Found at Highley Colliery.
Fossilised scales of the Devonian lung fish Dipterus sp.
Fossilised remains of the ventral headshield of the Silurian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devil's Hole, Morville
Fossilised remains of a jaw from an extinct species of hippopotamus containing an incomplete tooth. Collected from the Siwalik Hills of India.

Fossilised shell remains of the Carboniferous articulate brachiopod, Productus sp. Showing a typical staraight hinge line. Fine radial ribbing and corrugated, concentric growth lines. Found near Castleton, Derbyshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Platyceras haliotis.

Silurian assemblage containing the fossilised remains of coelenterata, Coenites sp.; bryozoa, Fenestella sp.; brachiopods, Atrypa reticularis, Howellella elegans, Rhynchotreta cuneata and Anastrophia deflexa; trilobite, Acaste downingiae; cricoconarida, Tentaculites ornatus and graptolite Ptilograptus acutus. Found by Millington, S. at Wren's Nest, Dudley
taxonomy: Fenestella sp.; Atrypa reticularis; Howellella elegans; Rhynchotreta cuneata; Anastrophia deflexa; Acaste downingiae; Tentaculites ornatus; Ptilograptus acutus

description: Possible fossilised fragment of a Silurian eurypterid. Found by Wood, B. at Burrington.
Fossilised remains of the planispirally coiled Silurian gastropod, Platyschisma helicites
Fossilised assemblage consisting of remains of phragmocones of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. and Silurian graptolites, showing orientation. Found in the Welshpool district.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian sponge, Clathrodictyon striatellum

Fossilised remains of part and counterpart of a large waterworn nodule containing a death assemblage of fragments of the phragmocones of the Silurian orthocone nautiloid, Orthoceras gregarium. Individual fossils display features including, suture lines, septal surfaces and septal foramen. Phragmocone fragments display a preferred orientation, indicating concentration and alignment by current action. Mounted on card.
Fossilized remains of Devonian fish Pteraspis leathensis found at Yew Tree Dingle, Morville
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. found at Burrington.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian coral, Sphenothallus ? Found at Builth Wells bridge.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Strophonella euglypha. Found by Bibbs.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopods,Dolerorthis rustica, Atrypa reticularis, Rhynchotreta cuneata and Leptaena rhomboidalis found at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
taxonomy: Dolerorthis rustica; Atrypa reticularis; Rhynchotreta cuneata; Leptaena rhomboidalis

description: Fossilised remains of cephalon and pygidia of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Upper Millichope.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian echinoid, Echinocystis pomum.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Amphistrophia funiculata. Part of the Bibbs Collection.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian agnathan fish Cephalaspis sp. found at Bromfield, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Kionoceras angulatum.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita discors.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ibex, showing septal divisions. Found at High Vinnals.
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras bullatum .
Numerous fossilised shells and moulds of the Ordovician gastropod Bellerophon perturbatus. Found near Vennington, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of fragment of the phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita globosa found in the Ludlow district.

Fossilised remains of a complete fossilised exoskeleton of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus gibbsi? The cephalon displays a fimbriated margin and inflated glabella, whilst the thorax has very clearly defined pleura.
Fossilised remains of a Devonian Cepalasid fish found at the junction of Stapley and Hudwick Dingle
Fossilised fragment of the phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Kionoceras angulatum. Found at Landeilo Hill, Radnorshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Monograptus sp. found at Elton.

Fossilised remains of part and counterpart of a large waterworn nodule containing a death assemblage of fragments of the phragmocones of the Silurian orthocone nautiloid, Orthoceras gregarium. Individual fossils display features including, suture lines, septal surfaces and septal foramen. Phragmocone fragments display a preferred orientation, indicating concentration and alignment by current action. Mounted on card.
Fossilised remains of a complete exoskeleton of an Ordovician trilobite, probably Cryptolithus sp. Thought to have been found at Hillside, Llandrindod Wells. Part and counterpart with G.02618.002
Fossilised remains of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod Orthoceras attenuatum showing chambers divided by septa into camerae. Found in Sunken Lane, Burrington, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Fuchsella amygdelina found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Calymene sp. found at Burrington.
Fossilised remains of the ventral head shield of a Silurian heterstracan fish, Phialaspis symondsi found at Devil's Hole, Morville.

Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Encrinurus punctatus and shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia nucula and gastropod Poleumita sp. found in the quarry below Mortimer Forest.
taxonomy: Encrinurus punctatus; Camarotoechia nucula; Poleumita sp.

description: Part of a Silurian fossil brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Dow, End Walsall
Fragment of a septal surface of a Leurocycloceras whitcliffense cephalopod fossil. Silurian. Found at Whitcliffe.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous plant, Alethopteris decurrens
Fossilised remains of fragments of the Silurian heterostracan fish Cephalaspis sp. found in Sheet Road, Ludlow
Fossilised remains of an adpression of a part of the stem of Carboniferous plant, Sigillaria, found at Highley Colliery, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites sp. showing the left genal spine and compound eye. Found at Wren's Nest.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Gypidula galeata found in Shadwell Quarry.
Fossilised remains of the cephalon of Ordovician trilobites found at Llanfawr Quarry

Fossilised remains of cephalon from the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus (a) and pygidium from the trilobite Platycalymene duplicata (b). Found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells. Counterpart to trilobite specimen G.04897.002.
Fossilised remains of Silurian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devil's Hole, Morville
Fossilised remains of a valve from the Carboniferous articulate brachiopod, Spirifer crassa. The valve has radial ribbing and a deep median sulcus.
Part of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense.
Fossilised remains of a single, incomplete valve of the Cretaceous Bivalve, Inoceramus concentricus.  The valve is inequilateral and displays strongly ridged concentric growth lines.

Fossilised remains of cephalon from the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus fimbriatus (a) and pygidium from trilobite Platycalymene duplicata (b). Found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells. Counterpart to trilobite specimen G.04897.001.
Mandibular border of an endogarth of a Silurian eurypterid, Pterygotus sp.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous crinoid stems in crinoidal limestone.
Pyritised remains of the Jurassic crinoid Pentacrinites fossilis. Remains of stems, branching arms and pinnules present.

Fossilised remains of one valve from the Cretaceous Bivalve, Chlamys sp. The valve is flat, equilateral and shows strong radial ribbing. Prominant 'ears' are visible on either side of the umbo. Collected from the Upper Chalk.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Kionoceras angulatum showing septal segments. Found in Wigmore Road, Ludlow.
Part of the phragmocone (cast (a) & mould (b)) of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ibex.
Fossilised remains of ventral rim of the Silurian Cephalaspid fish Hemicyclaspis sp. found at Temeside, Ludlow.

Fossilised remains of the ventral rim, of the headshield, of the Silurian Cephalaspid fish, Hemicyclaspis sp. Further fish fragments, including spines, may be seen within the matrix. Collected from within the Downtonian.
Fossilised remains of one valve from the Cretaceous Bivalve, Chlamys sp. The valve is flat, equilateral and shows strong radial ribbing. Collected from the Upper Chalk.
Fossilised specimen of Ludlow Bone Bed material containing many fragments of both fish spines and scales.
Fossilised remains of the ventral headshield of the Silurian heterostracan fish, Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devil's Hole, Morville, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian jawed fish Onchus sp. found at Ludford Corner, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of grinding tooth from the Cretaceous Hypodontian shark, Ptychodus sp. The tooth is cut by a series of pronounced ridges and grooves.
Part of the phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Polygrammoceras bullatum. Individual septa can be observed.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Grammysia cingulata.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian eurypterid found at Downton.
Fossilised fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Kionoceras angulatum, showing longitudinal ridges. Found at Bringewood.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi found by the Birmingham University Extra Mural Department at Hillside.
Fossilised remain of an assemblage of Upper Cambrian trilobites showing post burial deformation. Found nr Trawsfynydd.
Parts of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense. Siphuncular foramen visible on septal surface.
Fossilised remains of the Internal mould of the articulate Silurian brachiopod, Gypidula galeata. Both valves present. Pronounced fold and sulcus visible. Found at Elton.
Fossilised remains of the Devonian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devils Hole, Morville, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Niobella selwyni found by Norton, M. at Whitegrit Mine.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Onnia superba, comprising the cephalon only. Found in the River Onny near Wistanstow, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurain bivalve Fuchsella amygdalina.
Part of Silurian phyllocarid, ceratiocaris sp.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella debuchi found at Hillside.
Fossilised cephalon remains of Ordovician trilobolites Platycalymene duplicata (a), Trinucleus fimbriatus (c) and Cnemidopyge nuda (b) found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells.
taxonomy: Platycalymene duplicata; Cnemidopyge nuda; Trinucleus fimbriatus

Fossilised remains of Cephalon and other fragmentary pieces of the Ordovician trilobite Onnia superba. Found near Craven Arms, Shropshire.
Septal surface of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras sp. Centrally positioned remains of the septal foramen visible.
Fossilised remains of the jaw of a Silurian Acanthodian fish found at Aston Hill Wood.
Fossilised remains of the ventral shield of the Devonian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devils Hole, Shropshire. Figured specimen.
Fossilised fragmentary remains of Devonian fish - Phialaspis symondsi. Found at Devil's Hole "Arthrodire" channel.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras ibex showing the septal surfaces. Found in Lower Wood Road, Ludlow.
Near complete fossilised remains of the head shield of a Pteraspid fish Cyathaspis sp. Found in Morville area.
Fossilised remains of the head shield of a Devonian agnathan, Propepteraspis trimpleyensis found at Hudwick Dingle, Morville
Fossilised rermains of ventral shield of the Devonian heterostracan fish Phialaspis symondsi found at Devils Hole. Figured specimen.

Fossilised remains of the head and part of the body of the Devonian lobe finned lung fish, Dipterus valenciennesi, found at Achanarras Quarry, Spittal, Caithness. Individual plates are visible within the head section, whilst rows of scales can be seen within the body section.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Lingula lewisi.
Fragment of phragmocone of a Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil

Fossilised remains of pygidia and throracic segments of the Silurian trilobites Dalmanites myops and Phacops stokesi, found at Upper Millichope, Shropshie.
Carbonised remains of part of the trunk of the Carboniferous lycopod, Sigillaria sp. Mounted on card.
Fossilised cephalon remains of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Mortimer forest.
Part of the scaled body of the Devonian lobe finned lungfish, Dipterus valenciennesi. Individual scales also preserved within the specimen matrix.
Silurian brachiopod, Orbiculoidea sp. Found near Ludlow Castle.
Fossilised fragments of a swimming paddle of the Silurian eurypterid Pterygotus sp. found by Tarrant, P. & M. at Meadowley Wood.

Coxal segment with gnathobase of a prosomal appendage of a Pterygotus sp. eurypterid.
Fossilised fragments of Ordovician brachiopods Dalmanella horderleyensis, Cliftonia sp. and trilobite Broeggerolithus, in a course sandstone matrix. Found at Roadside Quarry, Horderley, Shropshire

Part of a Silurian fossil coral, Catenipora sp
