Polished section showing the fossilised chamber segments of the Jurassic ammonite Hildoceras bifrons.
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic bivalve, Cardinia ovalis. Found in the Whitchurch area, Shropshire.

Fossilized rostrum (guard) fragment of the Belemnite, Belemnites sulcatus, showing mould impression of a section of phragmacone. Jurassic period. Found at Wolvercote 1925. Part 2 of 3, counterpart to G.20046.001
Silurian assemblage containing fossilised fragments of brachiopods, graptolite, polyzoans and crinoid columnals. Found at Bromfield; transported in alluvial gravels.
Fossilised Silurian assemblage of brachiopods, Salopina lunata, Camaratoechia nucula and Protochonetes ludloviensis and cephalopod Michelinoceras sp. found at Ludlow
taxonomy: Salopina lunata; Camaratoechia nucula; Protochonetes ludloviensis; Michelinoceras sp.

description: Sedimentary rock composed of fragments of other rocks in a sand and mud matrix. Collected from the base of the Wrekin Quartzite in Ercall Quarry, this represents a period of erosion before deposition of the quartzite.
Fossilised Assemblage of  Silurian brachiopods Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula preserved as casts and moulds with little original shell remaining. Found at Whitcliffe.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian graptolite Monograptus lobiferus. Found in Lake district.
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic brachiopod Goniorhynchia boueti, preserved in boueti beds. Found near Weymouth.
Fragment of fossilised horn from an extinct species of Bovid from the Siwalik Hills of India. In 2 parts - see G.10029.001

An unusual mode of preservation for this Jurassic ammonite. An internal mould, which has been longitudinally sectioned, to show individual septa and septal walls. The septa are infilled with calcite. The exterior of the mould has undergone later solution to show zig-zag fissures, which mark the ammonitic suture patterns.
Fossilised fragmentary, indeterminate, Devonian plant remains. One large stem section showing longitudinal ridges.
Fossilised remains of Ordovician trilobolite Ampyx linleyensis found at Shelve inlier, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian bivalve Modiolopsis complanata found at Castle Bridge, Downton.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Pentamerus oblongus found at Norbury
Fossilised fragment of the phragmacone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras ludense. Specimen in two parts - part one of a two part specimen. Found at Tinkers Hill, Ludlow, Shropshire 1925
Fossilised remains of part of the stem of a Carboniferous lycopod Sigillaria, sp., found at Old Hill, Wyre Forest.
Fossilised remains of the rhabdosome of the uniserial, Ordovician graptolite, Dicellograptus cambriensis. A further fossil is preserved within the shale matrix, which may be an encrusting sponge.
Fossilised valve of the Ordovician bivalve Modiolopsis orbicularis, found by Darmon, C. at Glenburrel, Horderley.
Part of the phragmacone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras bullatum.Suture lines clearly visible. Small brachiopods also present within the matrix.
A pyritised shell of the Jurassic ammonite, Ammonites sp. The shell is involute and compressed and possesses strong transverse ribbing which passes across a ventral keel.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopods Heterothis alternata and Dalmanella sp. Found at Marshbrook.
Dark Ordovician slaty rock containing two species of fossilised graptolite, Dicellograptus sp. and Cryptograptus tricornis. Found at Llanfawr Quarry.
Silurian solitary coral Ketophyllum sp, overgrown by a colony of the tabulate coral Heliolites. Found at Elton, near Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian sponge, Amphispongia oblonga. Found at Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised head shield of the Silurian fish Hemicyclaspis sp. Found at Case Mills, Ludlow.
Fossilised valve of the articulate Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis. Found near Pipe Aston.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita discors found in Farley Quarry, Shropshire.
Fine-grained sandstone, Tarporley siltstone. One surface bears distinct ripple marks converging in a dip, thus giving the appearance of a whrlpool. The other surface is pock-marked (rainprints?)
Part of a leaf frond from the Carboniferous seed fern, Alethopteris sp.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita sp. found in Ludlow, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Heterorthis retrorsistria. Found by Wood, B. at Soudley.
Fossilised remains of pygidium and thoracic segment of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops found at Mortimer Forest.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian bivalve Orbiculoidea rugata found at Downton.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Dawsonoceras fimbriatum. Found in Mortimer Forest.
Fragmentary remains of acanthodian fish.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Leptaena depressa  found at Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised piece of a deer's lower right jaw, with teeth, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the trunk section of a Carboniferous lycopod Sigillaria sp. Found at Clipstone Colliery, Mansfield, Nottingham.
Fossilised remains of the  Carboniferous pteridosperm, Pecopteris sp. found at Longdon Common, Shrewsbury
Indeterminate plant remains from the Carboniferous.
Fragments of phragmocones of Orthoceras ibex, Orthoceras sp. and Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil with fragments of Serpulites longissimus, brachiopods and bivalves.

Silurian assemblage with corals Coenites sp,  bryozoan fenestella sp., and brachiopods Atrypa reticularis,  Rhynchotreta cuneata and Howellella elegans found at  Wren's Nest , Dudley
taxonomy: Coenites sp.; Fenestella sp.; Atrypa reticularis; Rhynchotreta cuneata; Howellella elegans

description: Fossilised remains of part of a Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena sp. Found by Kelly, F.B. at Woodbury Quarry
Fossilised impressions of raindrops in fine-grained Triassic sandstone, Tarporley siltstone. Found at Grinshill quarry, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of a vertebra from an extinct species of deer. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India
Fossilised Devonian plant remains found at Meadowley Wood.
Fossilised leaf remains of a Carboniferous pteridosperm Neuropteris sp.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian cricoconarid Tentaculites sp. Found at Rubery.
A sample of limestone rock with vertebrate bone fragments possibly a cleithrum bone. From the Rhaetic bone bed. Found at Aust Cliff, near Bristol.
Fossilised remains of Silurian Stromatoporoid overgrowing the colonial coral Favosites. There are also course grained fragments of crinoid stem and brachiopods. Found at Coates Quarry.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Kirkidium knightii. Found by Jones, M. on Mortimer Forest road, Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of an Oxynoticeras sp. ammonite from the Jurassic. Found at Seatown.
Rounded specimen of the Carboniferous coral, Lithostrotion sp. Probably a transported object originally from Angelsey.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian cricoconarida Cornulites serpularius found by Fraser, N. at Whitcliffe, Ludlow. Counterpart of G.16411.002.

Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Macrocoelia expansa, preserved as an internal mould. Also includes numerous ossicles of Glytptocrinus sp. Found by Wood, B. at Chatwall.
Silurian fossil brachiopods. Protochonetes ludloviensis, Salopina lunata and Microsphaeridiorhynchus nucula as well as fossil cricoconarida, Cornulites serpularius. Collected on Whitcliffe Common.
taxonomy: Protochonetes ludloviensis; Salopina lunata; Microsphaeridiorhynchus nucula; Cornulites serpularius

description: Fossilised remains of a part of the lower left jaw and three molars of an extinct type of horse from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita discors. Found by Norton, J. at Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised remains of head-shield of the Silurian jawless cephalaspid fish Hemicyclaspis murchisoni Found at Cusop.
Fossilised remains of Carbonifeous coral Lonsdaelia sp. found in Tyne & Wear
Fossilised remains of the Silurian coral Ketophyllum turbinatum found at Farley Quarry, Much Wenlock.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod, Pleurotomaria undata. Found at Leintwardine, Shropshire.
Fine grained precambrian sandstone showing fine sedimentary banding
Fossilised fragments of a Silurian brachiopod, Ludfordina pixis and a trilobite Dalmanites myops. Found by Kelly, F.B.

description: Fossilised remains of a Devonian fish.

Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Microsphaeridiorynchia nucula found in the Bishop's Castle district.
Fossilised remains of at least three bones including a talus and possible tibia from a large unidentifed species of ruminant, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Pentamerus oblongus found at Norbury.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone from the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras bullatum found in Ludlow.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Trinucleus sp. found at Bushmoor, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of an assemblage of fossils including the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Also includes tentaculites and remains of bryozoa. Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised remains of pectoral spine of a Silurian Acanthodian Cephalaspid fish. Found at Cusop.
Weathered piece of Bridgnorth Sandstone.

Fossilised remains of the Devonian fish Protaspis sp. found near Besom Farm.

Fossilised rostrum from a Devonian fish.
Part of Phragmocone of a Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil.

A death assemblage, mainly consisting of Silurian brachiopods, including, Leptaena sp. Atrypa sp. and Rhynchonellids.  Fragments of bryozoa and crinoids also present.
Fossilised remains of the lower right jaw of an extinct type of elephant, Elephas namadicus. Found in the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous coral, Syringopora geniculata, found in Northumberland
Fossilised remains of part of the zygomatic arch and maxilla of an extinct mammal species of mastodon. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Sample of dark greenish-grey medium-grained Precambrian Uriconian igneous Dolerite rock. Weathered to a dark reddish-brown. Collected from an intrusion on Caer Caradoc.
Fossilised fragments of indeterminate Devonian plant material. Found at Targrove Quarry, Downton, Ludlow
Fossilised fish fragments comprising Turinea pagei denticles,  Acanthodian scales and Pteraspid fragments.
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic Bivalve, Nuculana ovum. The shell is strongly asymmetrical and shows fine concentric growth lines.
Jurassic fossil nautiloid; probably Cenoceras sp. The specimen has been cut and shows the internal structure of the original shell.
Fossilised remains of part of the lower right jaw of a horse with three complete incisors, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Preserved remains of an assemblage of Carboniferous plant remains including fern fronds, seed fern leaves and cordaite stem. Found at Chorely near Highley.
Fossilised fragments of indeterminate Devonian plant material found at Targrove Quarry, Downton Hall, Ludlow

Fossilised fragment from a Carboniferous reef knoll, containing the solitary Rugose coral, Lithostrotion junceum. The corallites are fasiculate and phaceloid.
Part of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Cyrtoceras sp.

A sample of fine-grained green/grey sandstone (impure marble). The greenish colour is due to glauconite (an iron/potassium silicate) cementing the quartz sand grains together. A marine sandstone, part of the Lower Comley Sandstones. Collected from Ercall Quarry.
Fossilised remains of Silurian Rugose coral, Streptelasma sp. found at Rubery.
Fossilised remains of part of the lower jaw and teeth of an extinct bison from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilized fragment of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. showing chamber sections in the phragmocone. Specimen in 4 parts - part 4. Found at Tinkers Hill 1925
Coarse sandstone conglomerate. Found at Hope Bowdler unconformity.
Fossilised remains of a Carboniferous pteridosperm, Neuropteris sp.

Fossilised fragments of phragmocones of Orthoceras bullatum, Litustis ibex and Kionoceras angulatum as well as phragmacone fragments and septal surfaces of other orthoceras. Also includes examples of Goniophora cymbaeformis, Nuculites sp., a eurypterid fragment, Microsphaeridiorhychia nucula, an indet phyllocarid, Protochonetes ludloviensis, Orbiculoidea rugata and a bryzoan.
taxonomy: Orthoceras bullatum; Listutis ibex; Kionoceras angulatum; Goniophora cymbaeformis; Nuculites sp.; Microsphaeridiorhynchia nucula; Protochonetes ludloviensis; Orbiculoidea rugata

description: Fossilised remains of the Silurian coral Heliolites megastoma found on Wenlock Edge, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Microsphaeridiorynchia nucula. Found at Whitcliffe.
Fossilised and very near complete, finely detailed, impression of a Tertiary bony fish. Preserved in a thin sheet of lignite or brown coal. Found in San Paulo, Brazil.
Ordovician graptolites, Dicellograptus sp. preserved in shale. Found at Llanfawr Quarry, LLandrindod Wells.
Internal mould of the phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Imbricatoceras subundulatum, showing individual septa. Complete
Fossilised remains of corallum of the Silurian, solitary, rugose coral, Ketophyllum turbinatum.  The deep calice shows well preserved radial septa. Found on Wenlock Edge, Shropshire.
Possible tarsal bone from the foot of an extinct giraffid, Sivatherium giganteum, from the Siwalik Hills of India.
A sample of Jurassic oolitic limestone containing fossil fragments and a calcite vein. Collected from Leckhampton Hill, Cheltenham.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Kirkidium knightii.
Fossilised shell remains of a Teritiary gastropod found in grey fine clay, Water Lane, Walford.
Fossilised remains of a Devonian acanthodian fish Ischnacanthida.

Fossilised remains of Silurian ostracods; right valve double ringed and left valve of londinnia sp.; left valve of frostiella and nodibeyrichia with thelodonts and bivalves. Showing unusual shell preservation. Found at Church Farm, Warwadine.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found at Ludford Park.
Near complete specimen of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella augustissima. Found at Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised remains of a segment of appendage of the Silurian Eurypterid, Pterygotus sp. Found at Brandon Hill.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian fossil brachiopod Trigonirhynchia stricklandii found on Wenlock Edge, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Complete Tertiary gastropod, Sycostoma bulbus.
A fossilised stem, Sigillaria mammilans, from a Carboniferous lycopod. Displaying longitudinal ridges and some small circular leaf scars.
Fossilised carbonifrous coral, Lithostrotion sp.
Precambrian greywacke found at Haughmond Hill.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found at Leinthall Earle Quarry
Fossilised shell remains of the Ordovician brachiopods Dalmanella unguis found at Marshbrook, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian bivalve Modiolopsis sp. with gastropod Turbocheilus helicites. Found at Castle Bridge, Downton
Carboniferous trace fossil of potential annelid burrows or tracks. Found at Clee Hill, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Stophonema grandis. Found by Norton, W.J. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Dawsonoceras sp. found at Buildwas, Shropshire.

Fossilised remains of an assemblage of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Single valves preserved mostly as external moulds. Specimen also contains tentaculites. Found in the Craven Arms district.

Thin section of Cardingmill Grit, a sandstone found at Church Stretton.
Fossilised remains of an assemblage of Ordovician brachiopods including Dalmanella sp, Heterorthis alternata, Harknessella sp. and Sowerbyella sericea. Found by Norton, W. J. at Soudley Quarry.
Fossilised part of a Silurian brachiopod, Eospirifer plicatellus found at Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised remains of the vertebra of an extinct type of elephant from the Siwalik Hills of India.

Fossilised plant remains of the Carboniferous ' horsetail ', Calamites sp.

Part of a leaf frond from the Carboniferous seed fern, Alethopteris sp.
Internal mould of the cephalon of an Ordovician trilobite- Autoloxolichas laxatus. Counter part of G.11206 and more complete. Found at Chuney pool, Acton scott.
Fossilised remains of the Jurassic bivalve, Cardinia ovalis. Found in the Whitchurch area, Shropshire.
Hand specimen of Silurian Much Wenlock Limestone. Wenlock Edge
Fossilised remains of an orbicular pinnule from the Carboniferous seed fern, Cyclopteris sp. Part and counterpart with G.12318.002

Fossilised remains of the Jurassic bivalve, Cardinia ovalis. Found in the Whitchurch area, Shropshire.

A complete specimen of the Jurassic Bivalve, Pecten equivalvis. The shell is near equivalved  and near equilateral. The short straight hinge line has two 'ears', one either side of the umbo. Strong radial ribbing. Collected at Blockley, Gloucestershire. Mounted on card.
Fossilised remains of a crocodile scute; a bony plate found in the skin. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous plant, Annularia equisetiformis, found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of Ordovician trilobite, Barrandia homfrayi, found at Shelve Inlier, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the leaf stem of a Carboniferous pteridosperm Neuropteris sp.  found at Woodsetton Colliery, Tipton, Staffordshire.
Fossilised remains of Ordovician trilobite Ampyx linleyensis found at the Shelve Inlier, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the major part of the left lower jaw, with molar and premolar teeth, of an extinct type of antelope from the Siwalik Hills of India.

Fossilised Devonian plant remains found at Morville, Bridgnorth.
Microscope thin section of Permian Bridgnorth Sandstone. Found at Bridgnorth.
Fossilised remains of an assemblege of the Carboniferous non-marine bivalve Carbonicola sp.
A fine grained siltstone containing the remains of a segmented annelid worm.
Fossilised shell remains of the Ordovician bivalve Modiolopsis orbicularis. Found at Marshbrook.
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic bivalve Gryphaea arcuata, Devils toenail, found at Stratford Upon Avon.
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic bivalve Pleuromya costata.
Fossilised remains of tooth from the Carboniferous cartilagenous fish, Orodus ramosus.
Part of a fossilised root, Stigmaria sp. from a Carboniferous lycopod. Circular scars marking the position of former rhizoids, are clearly visible.

description: Hand specimen of Precambrian Andesite lava, found on the Lawley. The specimen has numerous vesicles many of which are filled with secondary minerals.

Fossilised exoskeleton of the Ordovician trilobite, Eodalmanitina destombesi. Preserved in slate and partially distorted.  The cephalon is incomplete but a well preserved kidney shaped compound eye is preserved, with individual lenses visible. The pygidium is near complete and posseses a posterior spine. ?  found in Portugal.
Fossilised remains of the compound  Rugose coral, Lithostrotion basaltiforme. The corallites are massive and cerioid. Found at Newton-by-the-sea.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Kirkidium knightii found in the Ludlow district.
Microscope thin section of a precambrian Andesite lave. Found on the The Lawley.
Fossilised remains of stem of the Carboniferous plant, Lepidodendron sp. showing diamond shaped leaf scars. Found on Clee Hill, Shropshire.
Thin section of Devonian sandstone found at Brown Clee Hill.
Fossilised remains of pygidium and part of the thorax of the Silurian trilobite Acastella spirosa. Found by Ludlow Junior School at Whitcliffe.
A single plate from the aptychi of a Cretaceous ammonite. This aptychus shows pronounced growth lines. Mounted on card.

Part of large phragmocone of an orthoceras ludense cephalopod fossil, showing several septal surfaces, with internal calcite crystallisation.
Fossilised single valve of a Jurassic bivalve shell
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena contermina Counterpart to G.09826.002. Found by Kelly, F.B. at Round Hill, Shropshire.
Fossilised coiled shell of a large Jurassic ammonite.

A series of fossilised ammonite shells, which are arranged in an overlapping and linear fashion. Thought to be an example of the regurgitated stomach contents of a Jurassic shark like fish such as Hybodus sp. An indeterminate fossil fish scale is also preserved within the shale matrix.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian trilobite Decoroproetus wigwig showing cephalon, thorax and pygidium. Found at Buildwas, Shropshire. Counterpart of G.11786.002
Fossilised part of silurian stromatoporoid, Stromatopora sp.
Fragment of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Dawsonoceras ibex. Named as Orthoceras pereligans on the original label. Found on Tinkers Hill, 1925.
Fossilised remains of Silurian tabulate coral, Heliolites sp., found on Wenlock Edge
Fossilised remains of head-shield of the Silurian Aganthan fish Didymaspis sp. found at Cusop.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician crinoid Balacrinus sp. in fine-grained sandstone. The matrix also contains remains of brachiopod and bryozoan.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian plant, Cooksonia. Found at Hopton Brook.

Fossilised clay concretion, containing the skeleton of an indeterminate Pleistocene fossil fish. The skeleton is near complete, with gill flaps visible in the head area. Individual vertebrae are clearly defined and fins are well preserved. Found in Greenland. Counterpart to G.20254.001
Fine-grained sandstone showing sedimentary structures. Grinshill Flagstone member. Found at Grinshill Quarry.
Silurian trace fossil, possibly burrowing structures attributed to annelids. Found in South Shropshire.

Sample of coarse grained intrusive igneous precambrian Granophyre, consisting of quartz (grey) and feldspar (pink and white). Similar to a granite but the quartz and feldspar are often intergrown. Collected from the Ercall.
Hand specimen of sandstone found at Grinshill.
A series of fossilised interwoven worm casts, collected from the Weald Clay on the Isle of Wight.
Silurian fossil brachiopods including Salopina lunata, found in Lower Broad Street, Ludlow.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian fossil brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.
Acanthodian spine fragment preserved in a course conglomerate. Found below the Psammosteus Limestone.

Fossilised impression of a single lanceolate leaf from a Neogene plant. The leaf is well preserved and shows a clear central rib with further veins emanating from it. Collected from beds of Upper Pliocene age, Willershausen, West Germany.
Fossilised remains of Upper Carboniferous horsetail, Asterophyllites foliosus. Found in Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of the Silurian cephalaspid fish Hemicyclaspis lightbodii found on the bank of the River Teme, Ludlow.
Hand specimen of dolerite found at Shelve. The weathered surfaces are brown coloured but the fresh face is a dark grey.
Fossilised remains of the main stem of the Carboniferous Lycopod, Lepidodendron sp.
Brown, weathered, Precambrian andesitic lava with gas bubbles (vesicles) infilled with radiating fibres of zeolite. Collected from Uriconian rocks on Caer Caradoc.
An internal mould of the root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria sp. Circular scars showing the position of individual rhizoids are well preserved.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp., Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised planispiral shell of the Pliocene gastropod, Helisoma sp. Mounted on card.
Specimen of shelly Jurassic limestone, containing an assembledge of embedded shell and rock fragments. Found at Bridport, Dorset.
Fossilised plant remains found at Sudford Dingle.
Fossilised remains of the foliage of a Carboniferous seed fern, Cyclopteris sp. from the East Shropshire Coalfield.
Fossilised pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Ogyginus porcatus. Preserved in a soft black mudstone.
A fragment of a fossilised whale bone from the Neogene period. Collected from Miocene rocks in Choptank, Maryland.
Fossilised fragments of the Silurian uniserial graptolite, Monograptus priodon. Found in Sunken Lane, Burrington, Shrophire.
Fossilised remains of a leaf frond from a Carboniferous pteridosperm, seed fern, found at Longden Common, Shropshire.

Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophonema grandis, Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.

Fossilised remains of a complete Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Bringewood
Fossilised shell remains of the cretaceous bivalve Inoceramus sp.
Fossilised shell remains of a Teritiary gastropod found in grey fine clay, Water Lane, Walford.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophomena grandis. Found by Norton, W.J. at Marshbook.
Fossilised remains of  leaf fronds of the Upper Carboniferous pteridosperm, Alethopteris serlii.
Fossilised shell remains of a Teritiary gastropod found in grey fine clay, Water Lane, Walford.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.

Fossilsied impression of the stem of the Carboniferous lycopsid Sigillaria sp. Found at Woodhouse Colliery Bank.

Fossilised fragmentary remains of numerous Monograptid graptolites. The reverse of the slab shows a bedding plane with numerous Lingula lewisi brachiopods as well. Found by Johnston, P.L. at Rowton, near Craven Arms.
Piece of Silurian sandstone found at the site of the Ludlow bypass.
Impression left in coarse sandstone by the bark of a Carboniferous lycopod, Lepidodendron. Found at Titterstone, Clee Hill.
Fossilised remains of a frond of the Carboniferous seed fern Pecopteris sp. Well preserved, veined pinnae are visible.
Microscope thin section of an aplite granite found at Meldon Quarry, Devon.
Fossilised impressions of the valves of the Ordovician brachiopods Heterorthis sp. and Sowerbyella sericea. Found by Stace, H. E. near Soudley.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous coral Lithostrotion sp. found at Llanarmon-yn-lal, Mold
Piece of biosparitic Carboniferous limestone from Oreton Quarry.
Fossilised fragment of a tooth plate from a Miocene ' Eagle Ray' fish, Myliobatis sp. Mounted on card. Found in Maryland, USA.
A fine grained, white, well sorted, siliceous sandstone with manganese dendrites, concentrated on bedding surfaces. Collected from Grinshill Quarry.
Medium to coarse grained sandstone. Found at the site of the new Ludlow bypass.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian graptolite Pristiograptus sp. Found by Norton, W.J. in Mortimer Forest, near Ludlow.
A coarse grained brown quartz arenite, consisting mainly of sub-rounded quartz grains, interspersed with larger, angular clasts of ferruginous sandstone and siltstone.
A complete and well preserved fossil of the Ordovician graptolite, Phyllograptus sp.The rhabdosome is scandent in form with clearly defined theca.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopods Othis elegantula and Orthis vespertilio. Found at Onney River, Bala.
A fine grained mesocratic volcanic rock with a porphyritic texture. Feldpar phenocryts present within a reddish groundmass. Andesite/Dacite composition. Found at Cardington, Shropshire.
Fossilised ripple marks in Longmyndian grey shales.
Mould of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Found at Minton.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Macrocoelia expansa. Preserved as an internal mould.  Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.

Fossilised fragment of the phragmocone of the Ordovician orthocone cephalopod, Alumethoceras oneratum. Individual septal walls and the position of the siphuncle are clearly visible. Found at Lanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells.
Partially fossilised mandible of the youngest of the juvenile Shropshire mammoths. Found at a quarry in Condover during excavation. Dated to approx 14000 yrs bp.
Fossilised remains of the rounded, segmented thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Homalonotus sp.
Spine of ancanthodian, Silurian fossil fish.
Fine-grained dark-grey sandstone consisting of quartz with some feldspar and fragments of other rocks. Collected from Lawrence Hill, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of tooth of Devonian bony Coelacanth Crossopterygian fish. Found at West Portished.

Fossilised assemblage of the Ordovician brachiopod, Dinorthis sp. Preserved as both internal and external moulds of disarticulated valves. Specimen also includes Tentaculites sp. Found by Stace, H.E. at Ticklerton.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Rhynchospirina sp. found on Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised fish remains of the anterior part of the dorsal disc, labelled as  Pteraspis rostrata, re-identified as Rhinopteraspis crouchi. From Morville Farm. Pridoli Series, Late Silurian
Fossilised remains of the rhabdsome of the Ordovician graptolite, Dicellopgraptus intortus. The rhabdosome consists of two uniserial reclined stipes. Mounted on card with G.11148.002 and G.11148.003
A coarse grained, quartz rich grit containing mica flakes, and in part cemented by ferruginous material. Also containing numerous brachiopod casts, both internal and external.

description: Fossilised remains of a partial jaw and tooth of an extinct form of elephant, Elephas, found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Eospirifer radiatus found at Wren's Nest, Dudley
Fossilised remains of the internal mould of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Onniella  sp. Found by Wood, B. Locality unknown.

Fossilised part of a Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found by Phillips, N.M. at Rubery

Fossilised remains of the rhabdosome of the Ordovician graptolite, Dicellograptus cambriensis. The rhabdosome consists of two uniserial reclined stipes. Mounted on card with G.11148.001 and G.11148.003
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Macrocoelia expansa. Preserved as internal mould of single complete valve and numerous fragments as well as bryozoa. Found at Soudley.

description: Fossilised remains of part of the thorax, and the pygidium, of the Carboniferous trilobite, Phillipsia sp. Found at Lower Scaleber, near Settle.

Assemblage of Silurian brachiopods and gastropods including Dayia navicula, Isorthis orbicularis and Bembexia lloydi, along with possible bivalve remains. Found at location 33, Mortimer Forest.

description: Polished section of the fossilised remains in Carboniferous limestone of the colonial coral, Lonsdaleia floriformis. From the Bibbs collection.
Medium grained brown sandstone, with potential rain print impressions. Found at Pen Milan, Powys.
Carboniferous dolerite cobble or set, quarried from the sill at Clee Hill.

Specimen of Hunsruck Slate containing the pyritised remains of numerous trilobites.

Fossilised remains of the Silurian worm, Trachyderma coriacea, possibly showing a sediment filled gut as ientified by Botting and Muir 2017. Found in Ludlow.

An almost complete guard of the Jurassic belemnite, Cuspriteuris tubularis. The guard is slender and flattened. At the anterior end, a large section of phragmocone is preserved. Collected from the Alum Shales at Black Nab, Whitby.
Fossil brachiopod, Discina striata. Silurian: Wenlock Limestone.
Fossilised shell remains of a Jurassic ammonite
Fossilised remains of the Silurian coral, Favosites sp. found in South Shropshire.
Well preserved example of the Cystoid Macrocystella mariae. Found at Sheinton, Shropshire.
Precambrian micaceous siltstone. Found at Haughmond Hill.
Microscope thin section of a quartz rich Cambrian sandstone known locally as Wrekin Quartzite.
Hand specimen of tuff from the Ordovician Stapeley Volcanics found at Stapeley. Specimen has been cut on one side to make thin sections.
Fossil Trilobite, Ordovician period, found at Onny River Section. Presented to Shrewsbury Museum by R.A. Buddicom Esq.
Hand specimen of Precambrian Longmyndian Greywacke found at Bayston Hill.
Fossilised Ordovician brachiopod, Heterorthis alternata. Found at Soudley quarry.
Fossil Trilobite, Ordovician period, found at Onny River Section. Presented to Shrewsbury Museum by R.A. Buddicom Esq.
Fossilised Cretaceous sponge within a flint nodule.

Fossilised remains of Cambrian trilobites Parabolinella triarthra. Remains of the Cyanobacterium Morania are also present as black markings in the matrix which is rare in these rocks. Found at Shineton Brook
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous rugose coral, Amplexus coralloides

Beige-coloured sandstone containing the fossilised remains of the vertebra, ribs and partial shoulder of the Triassic reptile Rhychosaurus articeps. This specimen is a part of a group of fossils donated to Shrewsbury Museum in the 19th Century, one of which forms the type specimen for this species.

Assemblage of Silurian brachiopods including Protochonetes ludloviensis and Salopina lunata. The matrix also contains some crinoid ossicles and indeterminate bryozoan fragments. Found at Tinker's Hill Wood.

Fossilised clay concretion, containing the skeleton of an indeterminate Pleistocene fossil fish. The skeleton is near complete, with gill flaps visible in the head area. Individual vertebrae are clearly defined and fins are well preserved. Found in Greenland. Counterpart to G.20254.002
Fossilised shell valve remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Trematis punctata (Sow). Found at Folly Bank near Enchmarsh.
Fossilised remains of a shell valve from the Cretaceous bivalve Hinnites sp. Found in Cambridge.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Harknessella vespertilio. Found at Cardington.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Rhynchospirina sp. found on Wenlock Edge.
Pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Dalmanites myops. Found at Affcot, near Craven Arms in the Wenlock Shales.
An internal cast of one valve of the Ordovician articulate brachiopod, Dalmanella unguis. Found at Marshwood, Shropshire. Counterpart of G.00055.002. Original Ludlow Natural History Society specimen.
Fossilised fish fragments from the Cretaceous Period, found at Hanover point, Isle of Wight.
Hand specimen of Old Red Sandstone from the Brown Clee area. The specimen shows clear green-coloured spots caused by the reduction of iron.
Microscope thin section of a Carboniferous crinoidal limestone found at Llanymynech.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Eospirifer radiatus found at Mocktree
Thin section of Carboniferous Dolorite, also known as Dhustone, found at Clee Hill.
Microscope thin section made from a hand specimen of sandstone from Grinshill.
Hand specimen of Carboniferous limestone found at Llanymynech.
Thin section of a precambrian basalt found at Caer Caradoc.
Fossilised shell remains of the valve of the Silurian articulate brachiopod, Strophonella euglypha, displaying fine radial ribbing.
Thin section of a Carboniferous limestone found at Llanymynech.
Microscope thin section of Ordovician ash, made from a hand specimen collected at Betton, Shelve.
Thin section of Precambrian Longmyndian Greywacke found at Bayston Hill
Fossilised shell remains of Silurian bivalve Pterinea retroflexa found in Ludlow Town.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Dinorthis sp. Preserved as internal mould of single valve. Also present is fragment of trilobite. Found by Wood, B. at Soudley.
Microscope thin section of sandstone made from a hand specimen collected at Soudley.
Microscope thin section of porphyritic basalt made from a hand specimen collected from a dyke at Grinshill Quarry.
Fossilised shell remains of Jurassic bivalves found at Whitby, Yorkshire.
Well worn fossilised remains of the Silurian gastropod Loxonema sinuosa. Preserved in a water rounded pebble. Found at Linney, Ludlow.
Fossilised shell remains of two Cenozoic gastropods, Cerithium sedgwickii. Part of the Charles Ashford Collection.

A polished section through part of the phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Orthoceras distans. With calcareous deposits within the individual septa and the siphuncle. Found in the Ludlow beds. Counterpart to G.02270.001
Thin section of Carboniferous limestone from Derbyshire.
Fossilied plant remains, including leaves, of Neuropteris sp. in Upper Carboniferous shale
Single valve of the Silurian fossil, Palaeopecten.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician trilobite Cryptolithus fimbriatus
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous coral Lonsdaleia floriformis found at Stearaway, Wellington.
Fossilised remains of part of the root of the Carboniferous lycopod, Stigmaria. Deeply indented circular leaf scars mark the position of former rhizoids.
Fossilised remains of the Cambrian trilobite Parabolinella triarthra. Found at Shineton Brook.
Fossilised remains of partial phragmocone of the Silurian orthocone cephalopod, Kionoceras angulatum. Also present, the cephalon of the trilobite, Dalmanites myops. Mounted on card.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.
Fossilised remains of a headshield of the Silurian heterostracan fish Phialaspis (Traquairaspis) symondsi found at Devils Hole, Morville, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of Carboniferous starfish Neupalaeaster sp. found at Plashetts Quarry, Kielder, Northumberland
Fossilised remains of stem and leaves of the Upper Carboniferous plant, Pteridosperm .
Part of the fossilised guard of the Cretaceous belemnite, Duvalia emerici. The guard is flattened and spear shaped in form. Mounted on card.
Fossilized remains of headshield of the Devonian agnathan fish Pteraspis sp., found at Aston Hill Wood
Fossilised shell valve remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Trematis punctata (Sow). Found at Folly Bank near Enchmarsh.
Downtonian sandstone slab used for roofing. A small hole has been made close to the narrower end in order to allow it to be fixed to the roof lath.
Fossilised guard of the Jurassic belemnite, Belemnites sp. The guard tapers towards the posterior end, whilst at the anterior end, a small section of the phragmocone is preserved
Fossilised remains of part of the jaw, with a double row of tooth sockets, of a Quaternary crocodile. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous plant Lepidophyllum sp.
Fossilised remains of the pygidium and thorax of the Ordovician trilobite Calymene sp. and shell remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Resserella paracyclica. Found at Gretton.

Microscope thin section made from a hand specimen of sandstone from Grinshill.

Fossilised remains of the tusk of a Hippopotamus from the Siwalik Hills of India.

A near complete fossilised shell of the Jurassic bivalve, Modiolus scalprum. The shell is equivalved, strongly inequilateral and displays fine concentric growth lines. The hinge line is long and straight. Collected from the Lower Lias. Mounted on card.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Pentamerus oblongus (J de C Sow). Found at Norbury, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras tracheale, showing the septal wall surface. Found in the stream near Mary Knoll House, Ludlow.
Microscope thin section made from a hand specimen of sandstone from Grinshill.
Fossil fragment of orthocone of the Silurian nautiloid cephalopod, Orthoceras bubundulatum. Found at Ticklerton Brook, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Ordovician trilobites Platycalymene duplicata (a) and Trinucleus fimbriatus (b) found at Llanfawr Quarry, Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic bivalve Pleuromya costata.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod, Pentamerus knighti (Sow.) Found at Craven Arms, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Poleumita centrifugus found in the Much Wenlock district, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of guard of the Cretaceous belemnite, Belemnitella mucronata. The guard is conical in form and displays a small part of the alveolus. Mounted on card with five other specimens.
A fragment of the shell of the Jurassic Bivalve, Gresslya intermedia. A fossil ammonite occurs on the underside of the specimen.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician hyolith Hyolithos sp. found at Garmston. Part of the Canon Wyley Collection.
Fossilised remains of valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Lingulella sp., found in Montgomeryshire.
Sectioned and polished sample of the precambrian extrusive volcanic rock, flow banded rhyolite showing pink felsic layers. Found at Overley Hill, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of shell of the Carboniferous bivalve, Auiculopecten plicatus. Found at The Winnats, Castleton. The symmetrical shell displays concentric growth lines and fine radial ribbing.
Single valve of the ordovician brachiopod Heterorthis patera. Found at Harnage Grange.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Strophonella euglypha. Found by Smith, R. at Wrens Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised valve of the Jurassic bivalve Arcomytilus asper, found in the Cotswolds. Part of the Bibbs Collection.

The fossilised shell of the dextrally coiled, spindle shaped gastropod, Fusinus porrectus, consisting of seven whorls.The shell possesses a circular aperture and a long, narrow siphonal canal. Ornamentation is in the form of both spiral and transverse  ribbing. From the Palaeogene Period. Collected from the Barton Beds, Hampshire.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian cephalopod from the Wenlock Limestone. Found at Malvern.

Fossilised remains of pygidium of the Ordovician trilobite Ogygiocarella terraceline. The pygidium displays very well defined terrace lines. Also preserved within the rock matrix are remains of Bergamia? sp.and ostracods.
Part of a Silurian fossil brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis found at Dow, End Walsall
Single complete valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Heterorthis patera. Found at Harnage Grange.
Brown micaceous sandstone containing the fragmentary remains of a number of graptolites.
Fossilised fragment of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod Orthoceras sp. found in the Onny Valley.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.
Fossilised reains of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. and trilobites Tentaculites angelicus and Brongniartella sp. found at Chatwall, Shropshire.
taxonomy: Dalmanella sp.; Brongniartella sp.; Tentaculites anglicus

description: Assemblage of Ordovician fossils including two complete but separate valves of the brachiopod Strophomena grandis as well as other brachiopods and tentaculids.
Fossilised remains pygidium of the Ordocivian trilobite, Calymene sp. found in the Onny River, Shropshire.
Fragment of phragmocone of a Cyrtoceras sp. cephalopod fossil.
Fossilised remains of the horn shaped corallite of the Cretaceous solitary coral Trochocyathus conulus showing the septal furrows and growth lines. From the Upper Greensand formation.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Silurian brachiopod Strophonella euglypha. Found at Shelsley Quarry.

Fossilised remains of phragmocone of the Silurian cephalopod, Orthoceras sp. showing the septal lines. Found in the Abberly district, Worcs.
Fossilised remains of the horn shaped corallite of the Cretaceous solitary coral Trochocyathus conulus showing the septal furrows and growth lines. From the Upper Greensand formation.

A fossilised death assemblage, of Silurian articulate brachiopods, Atrypa reticularis along with complete bivalved shells and separate brachial and pedicle valves. Crinoid ossicles, small fragments of crinoid stem and bryozoan fragments are also present.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Silurian brachiopod, Lingula lewisi, found in Ludlow Town.
A near complete fossilised exoskeleton of an Ordovician trilobite, possibly Marrolithus sp. The cephalon has a pitted rim and one genal spine is preserved.
Fossilised fish fragments from the Cretaceous Period, found at Hanover point, Isle of Wight.

Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Bellerophon perturbatus.
A fragment of a fossilised stem from a Tertiary palm. The fibrous woody tissue has been replaced by silica. Collected from Tertiary beds in Libya.
Fossilised remains of the moulted exoskeleton of a juvenile Ordovician trilobite, Asaphellus sp. Collected at Shineton Brook.
Assemblage of carboniferous plant remains including Asterophyllites sp and Sphenopteris sp.
Fossilised remains of jaw of a Silurian Acanthodian fish found at Little Oxenbold.
Fossilised shell remains of the Cenozoic gastropod, Cerithium sedgwickii. Part of the Charles Ashford Collection.
Thin section of a Carboniferous limestone found at Llanymynech.

Three internal moulds of fragments of the phragmocones of the Silurian orthocone nautiloid, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense. Straight suture lines, marking the septal walls, well preserved. One septal surface showing a centrally placed foramen.
Fossilised remains of the stem and partial foliage of an indeternminate Carboniferous plant. Found at Pack Horse Bridge, Lewis Burn, Northumberland.
Fossilised remains of cutwater spine of Acanthodian fish and other fish fragments.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Kjerulfina sp.,  found by Wood, B. at Acton Scott Quarry.
Fossilised remains of Devonian fish Pteraspis leathensis found at Yew Tree Dingle, Morville
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic bivalve Pecten sp. Four shell valves are contained within the matrix. Found at Montpelier,Bristol.
Fossilised remains of the spine of a Devonian Acanthodian fish found at Monkhopton.
A very fine grained, coarsely laminated, Devonian red sandstone. Collected from Caerfai, St. David's.
Fossilised shell valve of the Silurian brachiopod, Atrypa reticularis. Found at Coalbrookdale.
Fossilized remains of the Devonian agnathan fish Pteraspis leathensis found at Yew Tree Dingle, Morville

A medium grained micaceous, grey, siliceous grit.

Fossilised remains of thorax and pygidium of the Silurian trilobite Acaste downingiae showing the convex dorsal surface on one side of the matrix (a) and cephalon with glabella and eyes on the underside (b). Found at Wrens Nest, Dudley.

Microscope thin section of Precambrian Longmyndian Greywacke, made from a hand specimen collected at Bayston Hill.

An equivalved and near equilateral Jurassic fossil Bivalve. The outer shell is incomplete, revealling the internal sedimentary infilling. The shell surface shows concentric growth lines. The umbones are incurved. Collected from the Portland Stone.

Slaty Cambrian rock from the Ffestiniog beds situated in Borth. The rock contains the fossilised remains of the Cambrian brachiopod, Lingulella davisii. Brachiopods also highlight the strain that was undergone in the rock as the brachiopods have been stretched lengthways and shortened widthways distorting their original shape.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian segmented worm Protoscolex lata, found at Church Hill, Leintwardine.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod  Orbiculoidea sp.found in the woods above Whitcliffe, Ludlow, Shropshire.

Fossilised remains of the holdfast, and part of the stem, of the Silurian crinoid, Crotalocrinites rugosus.
Fossilised remains of the septal surface of the Silurian cephalopod Leurocycloceras whitcliffense. Found at Whitcliffe, Ludlow.

Fossilised remains of the Silurian cephalopod, Leurocycloceras whitcliffense, showing four segments of phragmocone occurring with numerous examples of the articulate brachiopod, Camarotoechia nucula and polyzoan fossils.

Spine of ancanthodian, Silurian fossil fish.

A broken flint nodule containing a cavity in which the fossilised remains of an indeterminate Cretaceous sponge can be found. The sponge is branching in form and displays numerous pores on its surface.

Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Pentamerus oblongus, found by Norton, M. at Norbury near Bishops Castle.
Fossilised remains of an asymmetric valve from the shell of the Carboniferous bivalve, Posidonia becheri. Fragments of both bryozoa and brachiopods occur within the limestone matrix.
Fossilised remains of a vertebra from an extinct species of mammal. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Atrypa reticularis found at Roadside Quarry, Mortimer Forest, Ludlow.
Fossilised fragments of the Carboniferous trilobite, Asaphus caudatus, contained within a limestone matrix. Found in Derbyshire.
Fossilised remains of a large mammal vertebra, probably from an extinct type of elephant from the Siwalik Hills of India.
Fossilised remains of a Silurian brachiopod, Leptaena depressa found in South Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of a Teritiary gastropod found in grey fine clay, Water Lane, Walford.

Sample of Dactite, a fine-grained leucocratic, intermediate volcanic rock with flow banding visible as darker streaks. Some larger feldspar crystals apparent. Collected from the Precambrian rocks of the Uriconian Group at Woodgate Batch, near Hope Bowdler.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian fossil brachiopod, Glassia sp., found at Upper Millichope
Fossilised remains of the Devonian trilobite Kayserops sp. found in the Moroccan Sahara. Preserved in limestone from the Djbel Issoumour area.
An internal mould of the Jurassic ammonite, Echioceras sp. The shell is serpenticone and displays typical ammonitic suture patterns. There is a ventral keel with a sulcus on either side.

A fine grained siltstone containing the very well preseved remains of the sponge, Plectoderma sp.  A series of parallel stauracts can be seen at right angles to the long axis of the fossil. Identified by Botting and Muir in 2017 and described as a potential holotype.
Assemblage of pyritised Jurassic ammonites. Found at Whitby.
Fossilised remains of a Tertiary palm stem in which the woody tissue has been replaced by silica. Collected in Libya.
Greyish green mica schist showing well developed texture.
Fossilised remains of an incomplete specimen of the Silurian gastropod Poluemita discors. Found at Farley Quarry.
Fine grained siltstone containing the fossilised remains of a branching form of algae as well as a partial orthocone fossil.
Fossilised remains of the Silurian brachiopod Microsphaeridiorynchia nucula.
Thin section of Much Wenlock Limestone found at Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised remains of a the Jurassic ammonite Hyperlioceras sp.
Microscope thin section of Permian Bridgnorth Sandstone. Found at Bridgnorth.
Thin section of Much Wenlock Limestone found at Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised remains of Jurassic ammonite, Dactylioceras commune, found at Whitby, North Yorkshire. The shell is evolute and serpenticone and possesses ribs.
Microscope thin section of a precambrian Andesite lava. Found on the Lawley.
Microscope thin section of a rhyolite found at the A5 bypass cutting through Overley Hill, Shropshire.
Fossilised fragment of the Silurian cephalopod, Phragmoceras puriformis. Found at Mocktree Quarry, 1925.
Fossilised remains of the rear part of the cranium of an extinct type of Hippopotamus, Hexoprotodon Sivalensis. Found in the Siwalik Hills, India.
Fossilised rain prints in Precambrian rock, from the Longmynd, Shropshire.
Microscope thin section made from a hand specimen of sandstone from Grinshill.
Microscope thin section of a porphyritic basalt found at Grinshill Quarry. The rock occurs as a dyke structure within the surrounding Triassic rocks.
An incomplete specimen of the Jurassic Bivalve, Gryphaea arcuata. Found at a depth of twenty two feet, during a well sinking, at Wollaston.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.
Fossilised remains of a single valve of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophonema grandis. Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
Fossilised remains of cone, associated with the Carboniferous Lycopod, Lepidodendron sp. and held within an ironstone nodule.
Fossilised remains of two separate valves of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophonema grandis. Found near Marshbrook.
Fossilised fragment of the Devonian fish Pteraspis crouchi found at Sudford Dingle, Monkhopton
Internal mould of the Ordovician brachiopod Dalmanella sp. Found at Acton Scott Quarry.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician graptolite, Pleurograptus sp. found at Llandrindod Wells.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Reuschella horderleyensis. Found by Wood, B. at Soudley.
Fossilisd remains of a Silurian bivalve, Cardiola interrupta, which is unusually well preserved with both valves present.
Fossilised remains of a Carboniferous coral, Palaeosmilia sp. Found at Alnmouth, Tyne & Wear.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian fossil brachiopod Camarotoechia borealis found at Wren's Nest, Dudley.
Fossilised stem remains of the Carboniferous plant, Lepidodendron sp. Counterpart to G.14287.001
Fossilised pshell remains of the Silurian brachiopod Rhynchospirina sp. found on Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised shell remains of the Jurassic bivalve Pleuromya costata.
Parts of  Silurian fossil brachiopods, Pentamerus oblongus and Costistricklandia lirata
Fossilised remains of cephalon of the Ordocivian trilobite, Calymene sp. found in the Onny River, Shropshire.
Single valve of a Jurassic bivalve.
Fossilised part of a Silurian brachiopod, Meristina sp.
Fossilised and fragmentary remains of the Ordovician hyolithid Hyolithus reversus preserved in a fine grained sandstone. Found near Acton Burnell.
Fossilised shell remains of the Cretaceous bivalve Cardium sp. preserved in chalk.
Fragmentary remains of the leaf fronds of the Carboniferous tree-fern, Pecopteris sp. Individual veined pinnae, well preserved. Collected from the Coal Measures.
Fossilised shell remains of the Silurian gastropod Diaphorostoma cornutum (Hisinger). Found at Wenlock, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of the horn shaped corallite of the Cretaceous solitary coral Trochocyathus conulus showing the septal furrows and growth lines. From the Upper Greensand formation.
Triassic fine-grained sandstone found at Grinshill quarry.
Polished section showing the fossilised remains of the Carboniferous coral, Lonsdaleia floriformis. Found at Little Wenlock, Shropshire.

A complete, and well preserved, fossilised skeleton of the Palaeogene bony fish, Knightia sp. Fins, vertebrae, jaws and ribs, clearly discernable. Collected from the Green River Formation in Wyoming, U.S.A.
Microscope thin section of a quartz rich Ordovician sandstone known locally as Stiperstones quartzite.
Fossilised specimen of the Ordovician brachiopod Lingula attenuata. Found near Rorrington.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.

Fossilised remains of a strongly convex valve from the shell of the Carboniferous articulate brachiopod, Dictyclostus semireticularis. Fine radial ribbing and corrugated, concentric growth lines are clearly visible.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.
Fossilised remains of the Eocene scaphopod, tusk shell, Antalis striata.
Thin section of dolerite found at Caer Caradoc.
Sectioned and polished sample of the precambrian extrusive volcanic rock, flow banded rhyolite showing pink felsic layers. Found at Overley Hill, Shropshire.

The fossilised remains of the sponge, Plectoderma sp. showing a series of stauracts at ninety degrees to the long axis of the fossil. Both brachiopod and bryozoa remains can be found within the same specimen.
Almost complete Ordovician fossil trilobite. Found at Onny River Section. Presented to Shrewsbury Museum by R.A. Buddicom Esq.
Hand specimen of Carboniferous Clee Hill Dolerite.
Thin section of Much Wenlock Limestone found at Wenlock Edge.
Thin section of Silurian Much Wenlock Limestone found at Wenlock Edge.
Microscope thin section of Permian Bridgnorth Sandstone. Found at Bridgnorth.
Microscope thin section of a Carboniferous crinoidal limestone found at Llanymynech.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopods Sowerbyella sericea. Preserved in course sandy sediment, mostly as casts and moulds with no original shell. Found near Craven Arms.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Macrocoelia expansa. Preserved as external mould of dissarticulated valve. Found by Norton, W,J. at Soudley Quarry.
Fossilised remains of the Ordovician brachiopods Kjerulfina sp., Reuschella sp.,  and Sowerbyella sericea. Found by Wood, B. at Marshbrook.
taxonomy: Kjerulfina sp.; Reuschella sp.; Sowerbyella sericea

description: Fossilised remains of the Silurian colonial coral, Arachnophyllum sp., found in Coates Quarry, on Wenlock Edge.
Fossilised fragments of carboniferous crinoid columnals, and numerous brachiopods, contained within a limestone matrix. Found in glacial gravels.
Fossilised stem remains of the Carboniferous plant, Lepidodendron sp. Found in the Forest of Dean.
Fossilised remains of the Carboniferous ' horsetail 'plant, Calamites sp. found at Longden, Shropshire.
Single valve of a Jurassic bivalve Liostrea delta. Found at Osmington.
Fossilised guard of a Cretaceous belemnite, Belemnitella mucronata. Found near Arminghall, Norfolk.
Fossilised remains of a Jurassic ammonite clearly showing the septa walls separating the chambers in the phragmacone.

Fossilised remains of the horn shaped corallite of the Cretaceous solitary coral Trochocyathus conulus, viewed from the apical end of the corallite, showing the vertical septal furrows. From the Upper Greensand formation.
Thin section of Precambrian Longmyndian Greywacke found at Bayston Hill
Microscope thin section of Carboniferous limestone. Made from a hand specimen collected at Llanymynech.
Microscope thin section of Teschenite, made from a loose block collected at Titterstone Clee Hill.
Microscope thin section of Criggion Dolerite, made from a hand specimen collected at Criggion.
Fossilised shell valves of the Carboniferous brachiopod, Linoproductus hemisphericus. Found at Little Wenlock, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Ordovician brachiopod Strophomena grandis found at Marshbrook, Shropshire.
Fossilised shell remains of the Carboniferous brachiopod Spirifera pennystenensis found at Coalbrookdale, Shropshire.
Fossilised remains of leaves and fronds of the Carboniferous plant Pecopteris miltoni, found at Longden Common, Shropshire.
Microscope thin section of an oolitic limestone found in the Cotswolds.
